home is when you're three episodes deep on the couch and it quietly walks over and rests its chin on your thigh.

Eventually you understand:
the place you bought, the apartment you rent — those are just rooms.
It's the small creature waiting inside that turns the rooms into a home.

Only dog people understand:
addresses change, furniture moves, cities can be swapped,
but as long as you can still hear those little paws pattering toward you from across the floor,
you are still home.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who's moved cities but kept their "home" beside them — they'll glance at the one asleep at their feet
variations: Advanced: one day you travel for work and stay at a beautiful hotel. Big bed, thick blanket, city lights outside. But you can't sleep. Because home isn't there — home is in that photo on your phone, the one of your dog waiting by the door.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗禁止上沙發但永遠在沙發上的日常，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
家規第一條：狗不能上沙發。

第一個月：你嚴格執行。牠看你一眼，乖乖跳下來。
第二個月：你只在牠跳上來的時候唸一聲，牠聽完繼續趴著。
第三個月：你回家，發現牠已經把沙發當成自己的床，連抱枕都被牠擺成喜歡的角度。
第六個月：你蹲在地板上看電視，因為沙發是牠的。

你後來才懂：
家規不是用來管狗的，是用來提醒你「曾經有過這條規則」的紀念碑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個「家規從第一天就破功」的主人——他會看一眼正攤在沙發上的那位
variations: 進階版：你買了一張新沙發，發誓這次絕對不讓牠上去。第三天，牠已經選好最舒服的那個角落，連你回家都要從旁邊繞過去。

en.content:
House rule number one: the dog is not allowed on the couch.

Month one: you enforce it strictly. The dog glances at you, hops down.
Month two: you only mutter at it when it jumps up. It listens, then keeps lying there.
Month three: you come home to find it has fully claimed the couch — even rearranged the throw pillows to its preferred angle.
Month six: you're sitting on the floor watching TV, because the couch belongs to the dog.

Eventually you understand:
house rules don't exist to control the dog. They're monuments commemorating the fact that, at one point, the rule existed.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose house rules collapsed on day one — they'll glance at the dog currently sprawled on the couch
variations: Advanced: you buy a brand new couch, swearing this time will be different. By day three, the dog has selected the most comfortable corner — and you walk around it when you come home.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗從不評判主人的純粹陪伴，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你今天工作搞砸了。
你跟主管吵架。你跟另一半冷戰。你開車回家的路上想：今天是不是全世界都討厭我？

你打開門。

牠沒有問你今天過得怎樣，沒有問你業績達標了沒，沒有問你體重又胖了幾公斤。
牠只是衝過來，繞著你的腳轉三圈，把整顆頭塞進你掌心，尾巴搖到屁股都歪了。

那一刻你才明白：
世界上至少有一個生命，從來不在乎你是不是「夠好」。
牠只在乎你回來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給今天過得很糟的朋友——他會想起回家那一刻誰在等他
variations: 進階版：你蹲下來抱牠，眼淚突然就掉下來。你都不知道自己原來這麼累。牠舔掉你的眼淚，繼續搖尾巴——好像在說：「沒關係，你回家了就好。」

en.content:
You messed up at work today.
You argued with your boss. You're giving your partner the silent treatment. On the drive home you wonder: does the entire world hate me right now?

You open the door.

It doesn't ask how your day was. It doesn't ask if you hit your numbers. It doesn't ask if you've gained weight again.
It just sprints over, circles your legs three times, jams its whole head into your palm, and wags its tail so hard its whole back end goes sideways.

In that moment, you realize:
there is at least one living being in the world that does not care whether you're "good enough."
It only cares that you came home.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend having a rough day — they'll remember who's waiting for them at home
variations: Advanced: you crouch down to hug it, and suddenly the tears come. You didn't even know you were that exhausted. It licks the tears off your face and keeps wagging — as if to say, "it's okay. You're home now."

items.sourceNote: 狗毛沾全身但本狗依然蓬鬆的物理悖論，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出門前花了五分鐘用滾筒黏狗毛。

上衣黏完——還有。
褲子黏完——還有。
包包翻出來——裡面有三根。
手機殼背面——卡了兩根。
你最後放棄，認命走出門。

上班的同事看你一眼：「你家養什麼狗？」
你看自己一眼，發現外套袖子上又多了一根。

更詭異的是：你家那位掉了這麼多毛，本狗站在那裡依然蓬蓬鬆鬆，毛量看起來毫髮無傷。

養狗才懂：
狗毛遵循的是另一套物理法則——
牠掉的不會少，你身上沾的不會多，但宇宙之間就是憑空多出了無限多的毛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個出門前都要花五分鐘黏毛的人——他低頭一看就笑了
variations: 進階版：你穿黑色出門，地獄模式。你穿白色出門，地獄模式 PLUS。你最後發現：穿跟狗一樣顏色的衣服，是唯一的解。

en.content:
Before leaving the house, you spend five minutes lint-rolling dog hair off yourself.

Shirt rolled — still finding more.
Pants rolled — still finding more.
You look in your bag — three strands inside.
Flip your phone over — two stuck to the case.
You finally give up and walk out the door.

A coworker glances at you: "what kind of dog do you have?"
You look down — there's another strand on your sleeve.

The truly strange part: your dog sheds this much, and yet it's still standing there fully fluffy, coat looking completely untouched.

Only dog people understand:
dog hair obeys a different set of physics —
the dog never loses any, you never accumulate less, and somehow the universe just keeps producing infinite extra strands out of nowhere.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who lint-rolls before leaving the house — they'll glance down and laugh
variations: Advanced: you wear black — hell mode. You wear white — hell mode PLUS. Eventually you discover the only solution: wear the same color as your dog.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗活在當下教會主人的事，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠不知道明天會發生什麼事。

牠不知道下個月的房租多少，不知道你帳戶餘額還有幾位數，不知道你昨天躺在床上失眠到三點是因為什麼。

牠只知道：
現在陽光很好，地板很涼，你的腳在牠旁邊。
所以牠把下巴擱在你腳上，閉上眼睛。

你低頭看牠睡得心無雜念的樣子，突然發現：
你一整天都在擔心明天，牠卻把整顆心安放在這個下午。

養狗才懂：
你以為你是在養牠，其實是牠在每天提醒你——
你已經擁有的，比你以為失去的多很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近壓力很大的朋友——他會看看身邊那個睡得很安心的小傢伙
variations: 進階版：你後來開始學牠。下班回家不滑手機，跟牠一起坐在地板上、什麼都不想，只是聽呼吸。十分鐘後你發現：原來「放空」這件事，狗一出生就會。

en.content:
Your dog has no idea what tomorrow holds.

It doesn't know how much rent is next month, doesn't know how many digits are in your bank account, doesn't know why you were lying awake at 3 a.m. last night.

It only knows this:
the sun is warm right now, the floor is cool, your foot is right next to it.
So it rests its chin on your foot and closes its eyes.

You look down at it sleeping with a completely uncluttered mind, and suddenly realize:
you've been worrying about tomorrow all day, while it placed its entire heart inside this single afternoon.

Only dog people understand:
you think you're taking care of it. Really, it's the one reminding you every day —
what you already have is far more than what you're afraid of losing.
en.editorNote: Send to a stressed-out friend — they'll glance at the small one sleeping peacefully beside them
variations: Advanced: eventually you start copying it. You come home, put the phone down, sit on the floor with the dog, think about nothing, just listen to its breathing. Ten minutes later you realize: dogs are born knowing how to do this. We had to learn.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗做壞事前先偷瞄主人的心虛眼神觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你坐在沙發上看電視。

牠走到桌子旁邊，停下來。
抬頭，看你一眼。
你沒有反應。

牠把前腳輕輕搭上桌子。
再回頭，看你一眼。
你還是沒講話。

牠的鼻子靠近那塊沒收好的麵包——
眼睛卻一直盯著你，像在做最後的風險評估。

你終於開口：「你⋯⋯確定嗎？」

牠瞬間把頭縮回來，假裝剛剛只是在欣賞桌腳的木紋。

養狗才懂：
牠不是不知道哪些事不能做。
牠只是想確認，今天的你會不會剛好心軟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都要跟狗狗鬥智的飼主——他想到那個試探眼神就笑了
variations: 進階版：等你低頭滑手機那一刻，牠會用 0.3 秒完成整個搶劫案。回頭看你的時候，麵包已經消失了，牠一臉無辜：「你說什麼？這裡本來就沒有麵包啊。」

en.content:
You're on the couch watching TV.

It walks over to the table and stops.
Looks up at you.
You don't react.

It lifts its front paws gently onto the table.
Looks back again.
You still say nothing.

Its nose creeps toward the slice of bread you forgot to put away —
but its eyes stay locked on you, running one final risk assessment.

You finally speak: "You... sure about that?"

It instantly retracts its head, pretending it was just admiring the wood grain on the table leg.

Only dog people understand:
it's not that the dog doesn't know which things are off-limits.
It's just checking whether today is the day you might let it slide.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone in a daily battle of wits with their dog — they'll picture that exact pre-crime glance and laugh
variations: Advanced: the second you look down at your phone, the entire heist completes in 0.3 seconds. By the time you look back, the bread is gone and the dog is staring at you with pure innocence: "What bread? There was never any bread here."

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到外出籠就知道要去獸醫的天線觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是把外出籠從櫃子最深處拿出來。

還沒打開，還沒講話，連看都還沒看牠一眼。

但牠已經知道了。

牠從沙發上彈起來，後退兩步，眼神先看你，再看籠子，再看門口——三秒之內把所有逃生路線評估完畢。

你溫柔地說：「來嘛，只是去檢查一下身體。」
牠用「我以為我們是家人」的眼神看你。

你蹲下來想抱牠，牠瞬間變成八公斤的雕像，四隻腳像生根一樣黏在地板上。

養狗才懂：
獸醫院這三個字不能講，籠子不能讓牠看到，連外出包的拉鍊聲都要小心——
因為牠的內建雷達，比你家的 Wi-Fi 還靈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次帶狗狗看醫生都要先演一場諜報戰的飼主——他光看到「外出籠」三個字就會苦笑
variations: 進階版：回程的車上，牠用「你怎麼可以這樣對我」的眼神看你三十分鐘。回家後消失在床底，三小時後才出來吃飯，但拒絕跟你眼神接觸到睡前。

en.content:
You just pulled the carrier out from the back of the closet.

You haven't opened it. You haven't said a word. You haven't even looked at the dog.

But the dog already knows.

It launches off the couch, takes two steps back, eyes darting from you, to the carrier, to the front door — escape routes assessed in under three seconds.

You say gently, "Come on, it's just a checkup."
It looks at you with the eyes of someone who thought you were family.

You crouch down to pick it up, and it instantly becomes an eight-kilogram statue, all four paws fused to the floor like roots.

Only dog people understand:
you can't say the word "vet," the carrier can never be visible, even the zipper of the travel bag has to be opened in another room —
because your dog's built-in radar has better signal than your home Wi-Fi.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose vet trips require an entire stealth operation — they'll smile painfully at the word "carrier"
variations: Advanced: on the drive home, it stares at you with "how could you do this to me" eyes for thirty straight minutes. Once home, it disappears under the bed, comes out three hours later for dinner, and refuses eye contact until bedtime.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗向所有來訪客人收取點心稅與摸頭費的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
朋友來家裡。

門一開，牠不是在迎接你的朋友。
牠是在執行海關業務。

第一關：聞鞋子。確認對方今天去過哪裡、有沒有摸過別的狗、有沒有偷偷帶零食。
第二關：眼神鎖定。對方坐下後，牠會直直地坐在對方正前方一公尺處，動也不動，像在等通關密語。
第三關：徵收。對方一伸手摸牠的頭——通過。沒摸——重新坐定，繼續凝視，直到對方良心發現。

等朋友離開，牠才回頭看你，那個眼神是：「我今天又幫你查了一個人。沒問題。」

養狗才懂：
你以為朋友是來找你的。
牠覺得朋友是來進貢的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡常有客人來的飼主——他想到那個「徵收姿勢」就忍不住笑出來
variations: 進階版：對方如果剛好帶了零食，牠會記得這個人三年。下次對方再來，門才打開一半，牠就已經坐在門口擺好標準姿勢——歡迎光臨，請出示稅金。

en.content:
A friend comes over.

The door opens. The dog isn't greeting your friend.
It's running customs.

Stage one: sniff the shoes. Confirm where this human has been today, whether they've petted other dogs, whether they're smuggling treats.
Stage two: lock eyes. After the friend sits down, the dog plants itself one meter directly in front of them, motionless, waiting for the secret password.
Stage three: collect tax. Friend extends a hand for head pats — cleared. No pats — dog re-positions, keeps staring, until the friend's conscience activates.

After the friend leaves, it turns to look at you, and the look says: "I screened another one for you today. They check out."

Only dog people understand:
you think your friends are here to see you.
The dog is convinced they're here to pay tribute.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who hosts friends often — the mental image of that tax-collecting sit-pose is going to make them laugh
variations: Advanced: if the friend happens to bring treats, the dog will remember them for three years. Next visit, the door's barely open before the dog is already in position by the entrance — welcome back, please present your tribute.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡著的呼吸聲、夢境抽動與翻肚成為家中療癒場景的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點，你還沒睡。

手機螢幕的光打在臉上，明天還沒解決的事一件接一件冒出來。
你嘆了一口氣。

床尾傳來「呼——呼——」的聲音。

你低頭看牠。
牠四腳朝天，肚子毛在燈光下蓬蓬的，嘴角微微張開，偶爾抽動一下，像在追什麼東西。

你突然不焦慮了。

不是因為事情解決了。
是因為這個房間裡，有一個生命睡得這麼沒有戒心、這麼放心。
而牠之所以放心，是因為牠知道你在。

養狗才懂：
你以為你是在照顧牠。
但很多個失眠的夜裡，是牠的呼吸聲，把你從焦慮裡撈回來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近總是失眠、卻有狗狗睡在腳邊的朋友——他會懂那個被呼吸聲安頓下來的瞬間
variations: 進階版：你後來發現自己不開夜燈了，因為牠的呼吸聲就是你的小夜燈。一吸一呼之間，你才慢慢知道：原來「家」不是地址，是這個聲音。

en.content:
It's 2 a.m. You're still awake.

Your phone screen lights up your face. Tomorrow's unsolved problems keep surfacing one by one.
You sigh.

From the foot of the bed: a soft "hmnnn... hmnnn..."

You look down.
It's belly-up, tummy fluff catching the light, mouth slightly open, twitching every now and then like it's chasing something in a dream.

And suddenly, you're not anxious anymore.

Not because anything got solved.
But because there is a living being in this room sleeping this defenselessly, this peacefully.
And the reason it sleeps so peacefully is because it knows you're here.

Only dog people understand:
you think you're the one taking care of it.
But on a lot of sleepless nights, it's the sound of its breathing that quietly pulls you back out of the spiral.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who can't sleep lately but has a dog dreaming at their feet — they'll feel that exact moment of breath-anchored calm
variations: Advanced: eventually you stop turning on the night light, because its breathing IS your night light. In the rhythm of inhale and exhale, you slowly understand — "home" isn't an address. It's this sound.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗如何感知主人快下班、用嗅覺判斷時間流逝的研究觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
科學家後來才發現：
狗狗會用「氣味的濃淡」判斷時間。

你早上出門的味道，是滿滿一杯咖啡。
中午剩半杯。
傍晚見底。

當氣味淡到剩下最後一層薄薄的香氣時，
牠就走到門口坐好。

不是因為牠看得懂時鐘，
是因為牠把你「留在家裡的那一點點存在」聞完了。

所以你開門的瞬間，
牠不是「剛好」在那裡——
是已經算準時間，等了你一整個下午。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天回家時，狗狗都剛好在門口的朋友——他會突然鼻酸
variations: 進階版：所以那些你以為的「巧合」，其實是牠用全身的感官，把你的時間表記在心裡了。

en.content:
Scientists figured it out eventually:
dogs use the fading strength of your scent to tell time.

The smell you left this morning is a full cup of coffee.
By noon, half a cup.
By evening, almost empty.

When that scent fades to its last thin layer,
the dog walks over and sits down by the door.

Not because it can read a clock,
but because it has finished sniffing the last trace of you it had to keep.

So when you open the door,
it isn't "happening to be there" —
it calculated, and it has been waiting all afternoon.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog is always magically at the door when they get home — they'll feel it in their chest
variations: Advanced: so all those 'coincidences' you noticed? Those weren't coincidences. The dog memorized your schedule with its whole body.

items.sourceNote: 兩隻老狗凌晨守窗等女主人下班的真實故事，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨一點半，你終於開車進巷子。

抬頭看二樓那扇窗——
兩顆毛茸茸的頭，一高一低，已經貼在玻璃上不知道多久了。

你停車、上樓、開門。
門一打開，牠們才從窗邊跳下來，腳步有點僵，眼睛卻是亮的。

你蹲下來抱牠們，鼻子忍不住一酸。

你白天加班的時候，沒有人記得你還沒吃飯。
但這個家裡，有兩個生命，從你出門那一刻開始，就在等你回來。

而牠們的人生，剛好只有十幾年。
你每一次晚歸，都是從那短短的一生裡，挪出來陪你等的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近常加班、家裡有老狗的朋友——他會立刻決定明天早點下班
variations: 進階版：你後來發現自己會在每個紅綠燈前算一遍：「再十五分鐘就到家了。」不是趕著回去吃飯，是趕著回去——讓那兩顆貼在玻璃上的頭，可以下來休息。

en.content:
1:30 a.m., you finally pull into the alley.

You look up at the second-floor window —
two fuzzy heads, one tall one short, already pressed against the glass for who knows how long.

You park. You climb the stairs. You open the door.
Only then do they hop down from the window, a little stiff in the legs, but bright in the eyes.

You kneel down to hug them, and your nose stings before you can stop it.

When you were stuck working late, nobody at the office remembered you hadn't eaten.
But in this house, two small lives have been waiting for you since the second you walked out.

And they only get about fifteen years on this planet.
Every time you come home late, they spend a piece of that short life waiting for you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been working late and has an old dog at home — they'll decide right then to leave on time tomorrow
variations: Advanced: you start counting down at every red light: 'fifteen more minutes and I'm home.' Not because you're hungry — because you want those two heads at the window to finally get to come down and rest.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對主人半年未見後的猶豫認人瞬間，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出差半年回來，蹲在門口叫牠的名字。

牠停在三公尺外，歪頭。

不是不認得，
是不敢相信。

牠先嗅了嗅空氣，又往前一步，再嗅一次。
眼睛圓圓的，整個身體微微發抖：
「這個味道我認得，但他不是已經消失很久了嗎？」

直到你輕輕說了一句牠最熟悉的那句話，
牠才像突然解鎖一樣，整隻飛撲過來。

那一刻你才明白——
牠不是怕陌生人，
牠是怕希望落空。

所以當牠終於確定是你，
那撲過來的力道，
是半年來不敢相信的所有想念，一次補完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近久別重逢、或正準備出差的養狗朋友——他會立刻想抱抱家裡那一隻
variations: 進階版：你後來才知道，那三公尺的距離不是冷漠，是禮貌。牠在給自己一點時間，確認這次的奇蹟是真的，才敢全心全意撲過來。

en.content:
You come home from a six-month trip. You crouch by the door and call its name.

It stops three meters away, head tilted.

Not because it doesn't recognize you,
but because it doesn't dare believe it.

It sniffs the air. Steps closer. Sniffs again.
Eyes wide, whole body trembling a little:
"I know this smell, but didn't he disappear a long time ago?"

Then you say, softly, the one phrase it's heard a thousand times,
and something unlocks. It launches at you in one full-body leap.

That's when you understand —
it wasn't scared of a stranger.
It was scared of being wrong about hope.

So when it finally confirmed it was you,
the force of that leap
was six months of unspoken missing, paid back all at once.
en.editorNote: Send to a dog friend who just reunited after time apart, or who's about to leave for a long trip — they'll want to hug their dog immediately
variations: Advanced: you realize later that the three-meter pause wasn't coldness. It was politeness. The dog was giving itself one moment to confirm the miracle was real, before letting itself believe it.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到主人哭時的反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你今天哭了。

沒大哭，就是坐在沙發上眼眶紅紅的那種。
你沒發出聲音，因為你不想讓任何人知道。

結果牠來了。

不是衝過來，是慢慢走過來。
沒有舔你的臉，也沒有汪汪叫。
牠把下巴輕輕放在你的大腿上，眼睛抬起來看著你。

那個眼神不是同情，也不是疑問。
是：「我在。」

然後牠就停在那裡，不動了。
你哭多久，牠陪多久。

狗不會說「你還好嗎」。
但牠用整個身體告訴你：
「你不需要解釋。我也不需要懂。我就是要在這裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得不太好、但身邊有狗的朋友——他下次哭的時候，會主動低頭看看身邊那一隻
variations: 進階版：人類安慰人的方式是「想辦法讓你別哭」。狗狗安慰你的方式是「讓你安心地哭完」。這兩件事，差了一整個物種的溫柔。

en.content:
You cried today.

Not a sobbing kind of cry. Just the sitting-on-the-couch, eyes-getting-red kind.
You didn't make a sound, because you didn't want anyone to notice.

It noticed anyway.

It didn't run over. It walked, slowly.
Didn't lick your face. Didn't bark.
It just laid its chin on your thigh and looked up at you.

That look wasn't pity, and it wasn't confusion.
It was: "I'm here."

And then it stayed there. Didn't move.
As long as you cried, it stayed.

Dogs can't ask if you're okay.
But they tell you with their whole body:
"You don't have to explain. I don't have to understand. I just have to be here."
en.editorNote: Send to a friend going through a rough patch who has a dog — next time they cry, they'll look down at the small head beside them
variations: Advanced: humans try to make you stop crying. Dogs let you finish crying. That difference is a whole species worth of gentleness.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗等待主人的時間哲學，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出門前，牠坐在玄關。
你回家時，牠還坐在玄關。

你以為牠中間都在睡覺。
後來你裝了監視器才發現——
牠真的坐了一整天。

偶爾起身喝水，回來繼續坐。
聽到電梯聲就抬頭，發現不是你就趴下。
再聽到電梯聲又抬頭，再失望，再趴下。

人類的一天可以塞滿一百件事，
狗的一天只有一件事：
等你回來。

所以你下班，請走快一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天加班、覺得家裡沒人在等的朋友——他會立刻想衝回家
variations: 進階版：那不是無聊，是專注。牠用一整天等你，是因為在牠的世界裡，你就是值得用一整天等待的事。

en.content:
Before you leave, it sits by the door.
When you come home, it's still sitting by the door.

You assumed it slept through the middle part.
Then you installed a camera and found out —
it really did sit there. All day.

Getting up only to drink water, then sitting back down.
Lifting its head every time the elevator dinged, dropping it again when the steps weren't yours.
Lifting again. Disappointed again. Lying down again.

Humans can pack a hundred things into one day.
A dog's day has one item on it:
waiting for you to come back.

So when work ends, please walk a little faster.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who works late and thinks no one's waiting at home — they'll want to leave the office immediately
variations: Advanced: that's not boredom. That's focus. It spends the whole day waiting for you because, in its world, you are worth a whole day of waiting.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡時的表情與抗議，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
「來，洗澡囉～」

上一秒還在客廳追尾巴的牠，
聽到這四個字，立刻從地球表面消失。

你找到牠的時候，牠縮在床底最深處，
眼睛瞪得像快被綁架。

你把牠抱進浴室，牠四腳僵直，重量變兩倍。
沖水的時候，牠用全世界最委屈的眼神看你，
好像你不是在洗澡，是在處決牠。

結果洗完吹乾的下一秒，
牠衝出浴室開始發瘋甩水、繞圈、咬玩具，
滿臉寫著：「我又乾淨了！我是一隻全新的狗！」

養狗的人都懂，這個物種的記憶大概只有 30 秒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有怕洗澡的狗的朋友——他這週末又要上演一次「綁架現場」
variations: 附帶觀察：被討厭的不是洗澡，是「失控感」。當牠發現自己又乾又香、世界沒崩塌，30 秒前的悲劇就會自動歸零。

en.content:
"Come on, bath time~"

One second ago it was chasing its tail in the living room.
The instant those two words land, it vanishes from the surface of the earth.

You find it wedged in the deepest corner under the bed,
eyes wide like it's being kidnapped.

You carry it to the bathroom. All four legs go rigid. It somehow doubles in weight.
The second the water hits, it gives you the most heartbroken look in the universe,
as if you are not bathing it — you are executing it.

Then the towel comes off, the blow dryer turns off,
and it explodes out of the bathroom shaking water everywhere,
running in circles, attacking a squeaky toy,
face beaming: "I'm clean! I'm a brand new dog!"

If you have a dog, you know — this species has about a 30-second memory.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose dog hates bath time — they have another "kidnapping scene" coming this weekend
variations: Bonus observation: it's not the bath the dog hates, it's the loss of control. The moment it confirms it's dry, fluffy, and the world hasn't ended, the tragedy from 30 seconds ago resets to zero.

items.sourceNote: 老狗陪伴一生的時間刻度，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠老了。

以前一聽到牽繩聲就衝過來的腳步，
現在變成慢慢撐起來，先伸個懶腰，再走過來。
以前可以跳上沙發的高度，
現在會站在邊邊看著你，等你抱牠上去。

你發現牠白毛變多了，眼睛有點霧霧的，
睡覺的時間，越來越長。

但每次你叫牠的名字，
那條尾巴還是會搖。
搖得沒有以前用力，但很穩，很慢，
像在說：「我聽到了。我還在。」

養一隻狗，是用你的十幾年，
換牠完整的一生。

而牠從來沒有覺得這筆交易不划算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有老狗的朋友——他今晚會多抱牠一下，多陪牠走一條巷子
variations: 進階版：牠不是變懶，是把每一個剩下的動作都變得更慎重。因為牠也知道，剩下的時間，要好好用。

en.content:
It's getting old.

The footsteps that used to come sprinting at the sound of the leash
are now a slow push to stand up, a stretch, a careful walk over.
The couch height it used to jump onto in one motion
is now a spot it stands beside, waiting for you to lift it up.

You notice more white fur around its face. The eyes are a little cloudier.
It sleeps longer and longer every week.

But every time you say its name,
that tail still wags.
Not as hard as before. Slower, but steady,
as if to say: "I heard you. I'm still here."

Having a dog is trading your fifteen-or-so years
for its entire lifetime.

And it has never, not once, thought that trade was unfair.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with a senior dog — tonight, they'll hold it a little longer and walk one extra block
variations: Advanced: it's not getting lazy. It's making every remaining motion more deliberate. Because somewhere inside, it knows the time left is worth using carefully.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗跟前跟後的影子行為觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你去廚房倒水，牠跟。
你去陽台收衣服，牠跟。
你去廁所——是的，連這個牠也跟。

你關門，牠就趴在門外等。
你出來，牠假裝剛好路過。

你說：「我又不會不見。」
牠的眼神回你：「上次你說一下就回來，結果離開了八小時。我不會再相信任何承諾了。」

養狗的人都懂——
你不是被跟蹤，
是被一隻全世界最矮的保鑣 24 小時貼身保護。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有「影子狗」的朋友——他正在試圖把廁所門關上，但失敗
variations: 進階版：你以為牠是在跟你，其實牠是在巡邏。整個家對牠來說只有一個重要的點——你在哪，牠就要在哪。

en.content:
You walk to the kitchen for water. It follows.
You step onto the balcony to grab the laundry. It follows.
You go to the bathroom — yes, even there. It follows.

You close the door. It lies down right outside.
You come out. It pretends it was just passing by.

You say, "I'm not going anywhere."
Its eyes say, "Last time you said you'd be back in a minute, you disappeared for eight hours. I'm not falling for any promises ever again."

If you have a dog, you know —
you are not being followed.
You are being protected, 24/7, by the world's shortest bodyguard.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with a shadow dog — they're currently trying to close the bathroom door and losing
variations: Advanced: you think it's following you. It thinks it's on patrol. The entire house has exactly one point of interest, and that point is wherever you happen to be standing.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡前轉圈的儀式行為，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠要睡覺前，會先轉圈。

不是一圈，是三圈、五圈，甚至七圈。

而且每一圈都很認真。
墊子已經是平的，沒有草、沒有蚊子、沒有任何敵人，
但牠還是要轉。

第一圈：確認方位。
第二圈：確認沒被埋伏。
第三圈：確認剛剛兩圈是不是白轉了。

轉完之後，重重一聲嘆氣——
整隻趴下去，像剛完成一場重要的會議。

你看著牠想：
世界上居然有一種動物，連躺下都需要彩排。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有「睡前轉圈鬼」的朋友——他現在腦中就有牠剛剛旋轉那一輪的畫面
variations: 進階版：你後來才發現，這個習慣是幾萬年前留下來的——草地上要先踩平、確認周圍安全。所以牠不是在表演，牠是在替你巡邏一張早就沒有危險的床墊。

en.content:
Before lying down, it spins.

Not once. Three times. Five times. Sometimes seven.

And every single rotation is taken very seriously.
The bed is flat. There's no grass, no bugs, no enemies anywhere on the planet,
but the spinning must happen.

Lap one: check direction.
Lap two: check for ambush.
Lap three: check whether laps one and two even counted.

Then, one heavy sigh —
and it collapses down like it just finished a board meeting.

You watch it and think:
there is a species on this earth that requires a full rehearsal just to lie down.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog spins before every nap — they're picturing the most recent rotation right now
variations: Advanced: it turns out this habit is tens of thousands of years old — flatten the grass, scan the perimeter, then sleep. So it's not performing. It's running a security check on a mattress that's been threat-free since the day you bought it.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗半夜對空氣狂吠的場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點。

整間屋子安靜得像深海。
你睡得正好。

突然——「汪！汪汪汪汪！」

你彈起來，心跳爆表，以為小偷、地震、或末日來了。
你衝去客廳，全屋大燈打開。

什麼都沒有。

牠站在窗邊，毛還炸著，眼神嚴肅，
像剛剛擊退了一個你看不到的敵人。

你說：「你在叫什麼？」
牠看你一眼，搖搖尾巴，回床上躺下。

第二天你才發現——
是樓上鄰居的腳步聲，是 200 公尺外的機車，是風。
反正不是什麼大事。

但對牠來說，每一個聲音都值得保護你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給上週又被自家狗在半夜驚醒的朋友——他現在還在補眠
variations: 進階版：你抱怨牠亂叫，但其實牠把你當成全世界最重要的東西。在牠的耳朵裡，沒有「小聲」這件事，只有「需不需要為你站起來」的選項。

en.content:
3 a.m.

The entire apartment is quiet as the deep sea.
You are sleeping perfectly.

Then — "WOOF! WOOF WOOF WOOF WOOF!"

You shoot upright, heart pounding, convinced it's a burglar, an earthquake, or the actual apocalypse.
You stumble to the living room. Every light goes on.

There is nothing.

It's standing by the window, fur slightly puffed, eyes serious,
like it just repelled an enemy you can't see.

You ask, "What are you barking at?"
It glances at you, wags once, and walks back to its bed.

The next morning you figure it out —
upstairs neighbor's footsteps, a scooter 200 meters away, the wind.
None of it was a real threat.

But to your dog, every single sound deserved a full alert.
Because every single sound might've been the one coming for you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who got jolted awake by their dog at 3 a.m. last week — they're still catching up on sleep
variations: Advanced: you complain about the false alarms, but your dog has decided you are the single most valuable thing in the universe. Inside those ears, 'minor sound' doesn't exist. Only 'do I need to stand up for you or not'.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗偷襪子當寶物的行為觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你脫下襪子，丟在地板上，準備等等再拿去洗。

你回頭——襪子不見了。

你找了五分鐘，發現牠在沙發底下，
用前腳壓著那隻襪子，眼神警戒，像在守護一塊金條。

你伸手要拿，牠把襪子叼得更緊。
你說「給我」，牠側身擋住，眼神變成「不行，這是我的了」。

你最後妥協，讓牠抱著睡。

結果你才明白——
那不是襪子。
那是「主人的味道」做成的安心包。
牠不是在偷，牠是在收藏你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡的狗會收集你襪子、拖鞋、內褲的朋友——他剛剛又少了一隻襪子
variations: 進階版：你以為牠喜歡襪子，其實牠喜歡的是你。襪子只是因為長期跟你的腳在一起，所以最像你。對牠來說，那是最便宜、最容易藏、味道最濃的你。

en.content:
You peel off your socks, drop them on the floor, and plan to grab them later.

You turn around. The socks are gone.

Five minutes later, you find your dog under the couch,
one paw pressed firmly on the sock, eyes on full alert, guarding it like a bar of gold.

You reach for it. The grip tightens.
You say, "Drop it." It turns sideways to block you, eyes shifting to "absolutely not, this is mine now."

You give up and let it sleep with the sock.

That's when you understand —
it's not a sock anymore.
It's a comfort packet made of "owner's smell."
The dog isn't stealing. The dog is collecting you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog hoards socks, slippers, or underwear — they just lost another sock
variations: Advanced: you think it loves socks. It actually loves you. The sock is just the part of the house that smells the most like you, because it spent the whole day pressed against your foot. To your dog, that's the cheapest, easiest-to-hide, most concentrated version of you it can carry around.

items.sourceNote: 「狗占床」現象的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
床，是你買的。
床單，是你洗的。
房貸，是你繳的。

但每天晚上，你只能睡床尾那個小三角形。
身體斜的、腳是懸空的、棉被蓋不到肩膀。

為什麼？
因為牠攤在床中央，四腳朝天，姿勢像被誰下了麻醉。

你不是不敢動。
你是不忍心動。

所以你維持那個彆扭的姿勢一整夜，
隔天起來腰痠背痛，
但你看著牠，居然還覺得很值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每晚都被狗擠到床邊的朋友——他現在正用那個姿勢看這則訊息
variations: 感情狀態更新：床是我的，但實際使用權在牠那邊。我只是租了床尾那一小塊。
進階版：你買的不是床，是給牠的大型躺椅。你只是那個睡角落、負責付帳的室友。

en.content:
The bed: you bought it.
The sheets: you wash them.
The mortgage: you pay it.

And yet every single night, you sleep on that tiny triangle at the foot of the bed.
Body at a weird angle, feet dangling off, blanket not even reaching your shoulders.

Why?
Because your dog is sprawled across the middle, all four legs in the air, looking like someone hit it with anesthesia.

It's not that you can't move it.
It's that you can't bring yourself to.

So you hold that awkward pose all night,
wake up with a sore back,
look at that little face, and somehow decide it was worth it.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who gets shoved to the edge of the bed every night — they're reading this in exactly that position
variations: Relationship status update: I own the bed. The actual usage rights belong to the dog. I'm just renting the bottom corner.
Advanced: what you bought is not a bed. It's a giant lounger for the dog. You're just the roommate in the corner who pays the bills.

items.sourceNote: 養狗後狗毛無處不在的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，狗毛就變成你生活的隱藏配件。

黑色上衣？上面有牠的毛。
白色上衣？上面也有牠的毛。
剛從洗衣機拿出來的衣服？
打開那一瞬間——毛重新登場，像奇蹟一樣。

你帶便當去公司，同事說：「你便當裡有一根毛。」
你看了一眼，淡定地說：「喔，是我家的。」

你已經放棄了。
你已經接受了。
狗毛不是髒，是你身上的家族印記。
是無聲的「我有狗」徽章。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天出門前都會看到衣服上有狗毛的朋友——他今天又把那根毛當勳章戴出門了
variations: 進階版：你不是有狗的人，你是狗的人。狗毛就是你跟其他人類最明顯的區別——他們乾淨，你身上永遠有故事。
備註：吸塵器是好朋友，但你跟牠都知道，這場仗沒有結束的一天。

en.content:
Once you get a dog, dog hair becomes a hidden accessory in your life.

Black shirt? Covered in fur.
White shirt? Also covered in fur.
Clothes fresh out of the washer?
You open the lid and — magic — the fur is right back on top.

You bring lunch to work. A coworker says, "There's a hair in your food."
You glance down and calmly say, "Oh, that's mine. From home."

You've stopped fighting it.
You've fully accepted it.
Dog hair isn't dirt — it's the family crest on your clothes.
It's a silent badge that says "I have a dog."
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who finds a dog hair on themselves every single morning — they're wearing one like a medal right now
variations: Advanced: you are no longer a person who has a dog. You are a dog's person. The fur is the clearest difference between you and everyone else — they are clean; you always have a story on you.
Footnote: the vacuum is a good friend, but you and the vacuum both know this war has no end date.

items.sourceNote: 「狗頭壓腿不能動」的契約觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你本來打算起來：
倒水、上廁所、回訊息、洗碗、洗澡。

但牠走過來，「砰」——把頭壓在你大腿上。

那一刻，整個計畫作廢。

你不能動。
你不能離開。
你甚至不能調整坐姿，怕吵到牠。

膀胱在抗議，手機在響，水滾了。
你只能傳訊息給朋友取消：
「抱歉，今天去不了。狗頭在腿上。」

朋友回：「理解，下次。」

這是養狗的人才懂的法律條文——
狗頭壓住的部位，視同被司法凍結，禁止移動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次想出門都被狗用「頭壓腿大法」綁住的朋友——他現在膀胱很滿但動不了
variations: 進階版：你以為你在養狗，其實牠在用一顆頭管理你的整天行程。哪裡都不能去的不是你，是你的腿。
備註：如果牠還順便嘆了一口氣，那這項契約自動延長三十分鐘。

en.content:
You were about to get up:
refill the water, go to the bathroom, reply to messages, do the dishes, take a shower.

Then the dog walks over and — thunk — drops its head onto your thigh.

In that instant, the entire plan is void.

You can't move.
You can't leave.
You can't even shift your sitting position, in case you disturb it.

Your bladder is protesting. Your phone is ringing. The kettle is screaming.
All you can do is text your friend:
"Sorry, can't make it tonight. Dog head on lap."

Your friend replies: "Understood. Next time."

This is the unwritten law only dog owners know —
any limb a dog has rested its head on is legally frozen and may not be moved.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who got immobilized again by the head-on-lap maneuver — their bladder is full but their leg is locked
variations: Advanced: you think you have a dog. In reality, one small head is managing your entire daily schedule. It's not you that can't go anywhere — it's your leg.
Footnote: if the dog also sighs while resting its head, the contract automatically extends another thirty minutes.

items.sourceNote: 毛孩有五個以上小名才算合格，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠戶口名簿上的名字叫「小白」。
但你從沒這樣叫過牠。

你會叫牠：
寶貝、寶寶、毛球、傻蛋、笨笨、屁屁、肉肉、小帥、阿白、白白、白先生、白總、毛主子、小可愛、大魔王。

依照當下心情、行為、罪行而定。

牠犯錯：「白！」
牠來討抱：「寶寶～」
牠睡到翻肚：「毛球～」
牠偷吃東西：「白總喔——你又來。」

人家說，狗如果沒有五個以上的小名，根本不算養。

你低頭看牠——
二十三個。已破紀錄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給給狗取了一堆小名的朋友——他現在正在算自己叫過幾個
variations: 進階版：你發現狗的小名不是隨便取的，是你心情的鏡子。心情好——是「寶貝」。心情累——是「毛球」。心情無奈——是「白總」。
備註：狗自己會聽得懂哪個是哪個。你以為你在亂叫，其實牠分得比你還清楚。

en.content:
Its official name on the paperwork is "Snowy."
But you've never once actually called it that.

You call it:
baby, bubba, fluff, goober, dummy, butt, chunk, handsome, Mr. Snow, Snow Snow, Sir Snowflake, CEO Snow, the Boss, little cutie, the great evil.

The name depends on your current mood, the dog's current behavior, and any crimes recently committed.

It does something bad: "SNOWY."
It asks for a cuddle: "Bubba~"
It rolls onto its back asleep: "Fluffyyy~"
It steals food off the counter: "CEO Snow — you again?"

They say: if your dog doesn't have at least five nicknames, you don't really have a dog.

You look down at it —
twenty-three. New personal best.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with way too many nicknames for one small dog — they're counting them right now
variations: Advanced: dog nicknames aren't random — they're a mirror of your mood. Happy mood: "baby." Tired mood: "fluff." At-the-end-of-your-rope mood: "CEO Snow."
Footnote: the dog actually knows which nickname is which. You think you're just rambling — the dog has the entire system mapped out better than you do.

items.sourceNote: 「我家狗的直覺比我準」的識人觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你交了一個新朋友，帶他來家裡玩。

你介紹得很開心。
他禮貌、聊得來、會帶伴手禮。
看起來人很好。

但你家狗——退到客廳角落。
眼神冷冷的，尾巴一動也不動。
你叫牠過來打招呼，牠裝沒聽到。

你心裡升起一個微小的問號。

三個月後，那個朋友爆出一連串你完全沒想到的事。

你坐在沙發上，看著牠，輕輕說：
「你當時就知道了，對不對？」

牠看你一眼，慢慢走過來，把頭靠在你腿上。

從那天起，你決定——
以後不喜歡你的狗的人，連喝水都不能進門。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都用狗的反應判斷對方人品的朋友——他剛剛又用這套標準刷掉一個人
variations: 進階版：你不需要做大量功課，也不需要看星座。你只要看你家狗第一次見對方那 5 秒鐘的反應，比任何 MBTI 都準。
備註：如果你家狗對某人特別熱情，那這個人，可以留下來。

en.content:
You made a new friend and brought them home.

You were excited to introduce them.
They were polite, easy to talk to, brought a thoughtful gift.
Seemed lovely.

But your dog — backed into the corner of the living room.
Cold eyes. Tail completely still.
You called it over to say hi. It pretended not to hear.

A tiny question mark formed in the back of your mind.

Three months later, that friend turns out to be involved in things you never would have guessed.

You sit on the couch, look at the dog, and softly say:
"You already knew, didn't you?"

It glances at you, walks over slowly, and rests its head on your lap.

From that day on, you decide —
anyone your dog doesn't like is not coming through that door, not even for a glass of water.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who uses their dog's reaction as a vibe check on every new person — they just disqualified someone yesterday
variations: Advanced: you don't need a background check or a personality test. The first five seconds of your dog meeting someone is more accurate than any MBTI result.
Footnote: if your dog is unusually enthusiastic about someone — that person is allowed to stay.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛無所不在的厭世共鳴，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你把家裡掃了一次。
你把吸塵器拿出來，掃了第二次。
你跪在地上用滾筒黏毛器，掃了第三次。

半小時後，回頭一看——

地上又出現一條毛、沙發上又出現一片毛、你身上那件黑衣服已經變成灰色。

你終於懂了：
你不是養了一隻狗，你是養了一座持續產毛的小型工廠，二十四小時不打烊，沒有員工，也沒有寒暑假。

你只是它的清潔工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都在跟狗毛戰鬥的朋友——他剛剛又從嘴裡撈出一根
variations: 進階版：你不再追求乾淨的家。你只要求：今天的毛，不要比昨天的多。
備註：等你死心，你會發現——家裡每一個角落，都有牠的毛在替牠站崗。

en.content:
You swept the floor once.
You pulled out the vacuum and went over it again.
You got on your knees with a lint roller and did a third pass.

Half an hour later, you look around —

there's a new hair on the floor, a new patch on the couch, and the black shirt you're wearing has officially turned gray.

That's when it hits you:
you don't own a dog. You own a small fur factory that runs twenty-four hours a day, has no employees, and takes no holidays.

You're just the janitor.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend fighting the daily war against dog hair — they just pulled one out of their mouth
variations: Advanced: you stop trying to have a clean house. You just hope today's hair count is lower than yesterday's.
Footnote: once you give up, you'll notice — every corner of your home has a strand of fur standing guard.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡後爆衝瘋狂跑跳，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
洗澡前：牠用眼神告訴你，這是背叛、這是酷刑、這是末日。

你抱牠進浴室時，牠四腳張開，撐住門框，像在抗拒被押進刑場。

你打開蓮蓬頭，牠抖。
你倒沐浴乳，牠嘆。
你沖水，牠瞇眼，看著遠方——好像在原諒這個世界。

你以為這是悲劇片。

結果——浴巾一拆，牠瞬間爆衝。
從客廳衝到房間，從房間衝到沙發，從沙發衝到你身上。
邊跑邊甩水、邊蹭地毯、邊在床上打滾。

剛剛那個快被你害死的狗，三分鐘後變成派對主辦人。

你才懂——狗狗的情緒，沒有中間值。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次幫狗洗完澡都被甩得滿臉水的朋友——他現在頭髮還是濕的
variations: 進階版：洗澡前你是劊子手，洗澡後你是主辦單位。在狗的世界裡，地獄跟天堂中間只隔一條浴巾。
備註：你拚命想把牠擦乾，牠拚命想用整個家把自己擦乾。最後總是牠贏。

en.content:
Before the bath: the dog looks at you like you've betrayed it, tortured it, and personally signed off on the apocalypse.

You carry it into the bathroom and it splays all four legs against the doorframe, like a prisoner refusing to enter the execution chamber.

You turn on the showerhead — it shivers.
You squeeze out shampoo — it sighs.
You rinse — it squints into the distance, as if forgiving humankind one last time.

You think this is a tragedy.

Then — the towel comes off and the dog detonates.
Couch to bedroom, bedroom to sofa, sofa to you.
Shaking, sliding across the carpet, rolling on the bed, sprinting in circles.

The dog that was almost destroyed three minutes ago is now hosting a party.

That's when you learn — a dog's mood has no middle setting.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who got soaked again after bath time — their hair is still wet
variations: Advanced: before the bath, you're the executioner. After the bath, you're the host of the after-party. In dog logic, the distance from hell to heaven is one towel.
Footnote: you try to dry the dog with a towel. The dog tries to dry itself with your entire house. The dog always wins.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗不能上沙發的家規最後總是破功，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你剛把牠帶回家那天，立下家規：

第一條，沙發是人的，狗不能上。
第二條，床是人的，狗不能上。
第三條，廚房是人的，狗不能進。

三個月後，你坐在地板上，牠躺在沙發中間，把整張沙發攤平。
你在床的角落縮成一團，牠四腳朝天睡在你枕頭上。
你在廚房洗碗，牠就站在你腳邊，盯著你準備好牠那份。

你看著家裡的格局，突然發現——

家規還在。
只是條文上的「人」跟「狗」，不知道什麼時候被偷偷對調了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡所有家規都被狗推翻的朋友——他現在正睡在床的最邊邊
variations: 進階版：你不是放棄家規，你只是默默把家規重新解釋成：「人也可以上沙發，前提是還有位子。」
備註：當你開始懷疑這個家到底是誰的，那答案早就揭曉了。

en.content:
The day you brought the dog home, you set house rules:

Rule one: the couch is for humans. No dogs.
Rule two: the bed is for humans. No dogs.
Rule three: the kitchen is for humans. No dogs.

Three months later, you're sitting on the floor while the dog is stretched out across the entire couch.
You're curled up in the corner of the bed while it sleeps belly-up on your pillow.
You're washing dishes in the kitchen while it stands at your feet, supervising the preparation of its portion.

You look around the apartment and slowly realize —

the house rules are still in effect.
But somewhere along the way, the words "human" and "dog" quietly swapped places in the fine print.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose every house rule has been overthrown by their dog — they are currently sleeping on the edge of their own bed
variations: Advanced: you didn't abandon the rules. You just quietly reinterpreted them as: humans may also use the couch — if there's room left.
Footnote: the moment you start wondering whose house this actually is, the answer is already obvious.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗永遠用同一種熱情迎接你的細節觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你今天在公司被罵。
你坐捷運回家的路上，差點哭出來。
你進電梯時，鏡子裡的自己看起來像被人生抽乾。

你打開家門——

牠衝過來，尾巴搖到整個屁股都在跳，眼睛亮得像你是失蹤十年的家人，鼻子在你手上、膝蓋上、鞋子上瘋狂聞，好像確認你還是同一個人。

牠不會問你今天怎麼了。
牠不會說「你看起來很累」。
牠只是用全身告訴你——

你回來了，這樣就夠了。

你蹲下來，抱住牠，吸一大口牠頭頂的味道。

剛剛在路上沒哭出來的眼淚，這時候才慢慢掉下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給今天過得很糟、回家被狗救活一次的朋友——他剛剛抱著狗哭了三分鐘
variations: 進階版：人類分得清你是不是裝沒事。狗分不清，但牠選擇相信你回家就一定好——所以你回家也真的會好。
備註：狗不知道什麼叫情緒勒索、什麼叫無條件的愛。牠只是剛好把後者活成了本能。

en.content:
You got yelled at work today.
On the subway home, you almost cried in public.
In the elevator mirror, the person looking back at you looks like life squeezed them dry.

You open the front door —

the dog comes flying. Tail wagging so hard its whole back end is bouncing, eyes lighting up like you're a family member who went missing ten years ago, nose sniffing your hands, your knees, your shoes, as if checking that you're still the same person.

It doesn't ask what happened today.
It doesn't say "you look exhausted."
It just tells you with its entire body —

you came home. That's enough.

You crouch down, wrap your arms around it, and breathe in the smell of the top of its head.

The tears you held back on the way home finally start falling, quietly.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who had a brutal day and got resurrected by their dog the second they walked in — they just cried into its fur for three minutes
variations: Advanced: humans can tell when you're faking being okay. Dogs can't, but they choose to believe coming home means you're okay — and somehow that makes it true.
Footnote: a dog doesn't know what emotional manipulation is, or what unconditional love is. It just happens to live the second one as instinct.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗吃飯時站旁邊盯著看的日常觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你坐下來吃飯。

牠不叫、不跳、也不討。

牠只是坐在離你 30 公分的地方，端正得像在面試。

眼神鎖定你的筷子，從你夾起菜，到放進嘴裡，再到你吞下去——全程跟拍，一秒都沒漏。

你不給，牠不吵。
你看牠，牠眨眼。
你假裝沒看到，牠的眼眶開始泛出一種「我這輩子沒被人愛過」的水光。

牠不是在乞食。
牠是在用沉默，對你進行為期一小時的道德譴責。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次吃飯都被狗用眼神審判的朋友——他剛剛偷偷剝了一塊雞肉
variations: 進階版：牠不是真的想吃你的菜，牠只是不想接受「這頓飯居然沒有牠的份」這個事實。
備註：你只要心軟過一次，這場審判就會變成你後半輩子每一餐的固定節目。

en.content:
You sit down to eat.

It doesn't bark. It doesn't jump. It doesn't beg.

It just sits 30 centimeters away from you, posture upright like it's interviewing for a job.

Eyes locked on your fork. Tracking every bite from plate, to mouth, to swallow — full coverage, no frame skipped.

You ignore it. It doesn't complain.
You look at it. It blinks slowly.
You pretend it isn't there. Its eyes start glistening with the energy of a creature who has never once been loved in its entire life.

It isn't begging.
It's holding a silent, one-hour ethics tribunal against you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose every meal is judged by a small furry court — they just snuck a piece of chicken under the table
variations: Advanced: it doesn't actually want your food. It just refuses to accept the existence of a meal that does not include it.
Footnote: cave once, and this tribunal becomes a permanent fixture of every meal for the rest of your life.

items.sourceNote: 養狗後手機相簿全是狗的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之前，你的手機相簿有：

旅行的風景、跟朋友的合照、吃過的好吃的、自拍。

養狗之後，你的手機相簿有：

牠睡覺、牠醒來、牠歪頭、牠打哈欠、牠肉球的特寫、牠看你的眼神、牠剛洗完澡、牠跟玩具的合照、牠跟同一隻玩具不同角度的合照、牠在你腳邊但你需要從三個角度記錄。

你的相簿不是相簿，是一本牠的個人寫真集。
而你，是牠專屬的、二十四小時待命的、無薪的攝影師。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給手機快沒空間、但永遠捨不得刪狗照片的朋友——他剛剛又拍了一張幾乎一樣的
variations: 進階版：你還會發現自己拍的人類朋友照片，沒一張比得上狗的角度好看——因為你拍狗的時候，是真的有在認真。
備註：手機容量不夠，你會先升級容量，而不是刪狗的照片。順序很重要。

en.content:
Before you got a dog, your camera roll had:

travel shots, group photos with friends, food you ate, the occasional selfie.

After you got a dog, your camera roll has:

it sleeping, it waking up, it tilting its head, it yawning, close-ups of its paw pads, the way it looks at you, it freshly bathed, it with its toy, it with the same toy from three different angles, it sitting next to your foot but somehow requiring four photos to fully document.

This is not a camera roll. It's a personal photo book starring one specific dog.
And you are its full-time, on-call, unpaid photographer.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose phone storage is dying but who would never delete a single dog photo — they just took another nearly identical shot
variations: Advanced: you'll also notice your photos of human friends never look quite as good as your dog photos — because for the dog, you actually paid attention.
Footnote: when storage runs out, you upgrade storage. You don't delete the dog. The priority is non-negotiable.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對快遞員的態度反差，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
門鈴一響，牠衝到門口，全身炸毛。

吠的聲音大到鄰居以為你家進了強盜。

你打開門，外面是熟到不能再熟的快遞大哥。

大哥微笑，蹲下來，摸牠的頭：「乖乖。」

牠瞬間融化，四腳朝天，把肚子翻給他看。

門關上後，牠又恢復世界第一兇惡的姿態，繼續對著早已不在的快遞大哥狂吠 30 秒。

你忽然懂了：

牠不是在保護家。
牠是在維持一個「我有在工作」的形象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種狗對外人凶得要死、對你軟得不行的朋友——他現在點頭點到脖子快斷
variations: 進階版：這份工作沒有實際內容，但牠每天都很投入。
備註：牠真正的工作是讓你覺得家裡有保鑣，雖然這個保鑣會被一塊雞肉收買。

en.content:
The doorbell rings. The dog explodes toward the door, fur fully puffed.

The barking is loud enough that the neighbors assume your apartment is being robbed.

You open the door. It's the delivery guy you've seen four times this week.

The guy smiles, crouches down, scratches the dog's head: "Hey buddy."

The dog instantly melts, rolls onto its back, and presents its stomach for inspection.

The door closes. The dog immediately resumes its fiercest-creature-on-earth posture and barks for another 30 seconds at the spot where the delivery guy used to be.

And you suddenly understand:

the dog isn't protecting the house.
It's maintaining the appearance of being employed.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog terrifies strangers and dissolves around them in private — they're nodding so hard their neck hurts
variations: Advanced: the job has no actual content. But the dog is fully committed to it every single day.
Footnote: its real job is to make you feel like the house has a bodyguard, even though the bodyguard can be bribed with one piece of chicken.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗每天都過得像第一天認識你，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
人類談戀愛的時候會說：

「我希望我們可以永遠像第一天那樣愛對方。」

然後兩個月後，互相已經懶得回對方訊息。

但你的狗，每天早上看到你睜開眼睛的那一刻，眼神都還是第一天那樣。

第一千天、第三千天、那一天牠老得走不動了還躺在你旁邊——

那個眼神，從來沒變過。

所以這世界上最會談戀愛的，可能不是人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種一回家就被狗用「終於等到你」的眼神接住的朋友——他剛剛差點哭出來
variations: 進階版：人類學了很久才學會的「無條件喜歡一個人」，狗一出生就會。
備註：你可能會懷疑這世界上有沒有真愛。看看牠看你的眼神，你就有答案了。

en.content:
When humans fall in love, they say:

"I hope we can love each other like it's day one, forever."

And then two months later, they can't be bothered to reply to each other's messages.

But your dog — every single morning, the moment you open your eyes, looks at you exactly the way it did on day one.

Day one thousand. Day three thousand. The day it gets too old to walk and still lies next to you —

that look has never changed.

Which means the best lover on this planet might not actually be human.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog welcomes them home every day like it's been waiting a lifetime — they just teared up a little
variations: Advanced: the unconditional love that humans spend years trying to learn? Dogs are born already knowing it.
Footnote: if you ever doubt whether real love exists, look at the way your dog looks at you. You already have the answer.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡前後的反差觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
洗澡前的牠：

聽到「洗澡」兩個字直接消失，找遍全家最後在沙發底下找到，眼神控訴你叛變了革命。

洗澡中的牠：

全身濕透、毛貼平、體積縮水一半，像被人生抽乾，眼神絕望，彷彿你正在親手結束牠光輝燦爛的一生。

洗澡後的牠：

吹乾後變成一顆蓬鬆的雲，在地毯上瘋狂打滾、繞著沙發跑八圈、用屁股蹭你的腳，那種開心程度，像剛被釋放出獄。

同一隻狗，同一個小時，三種人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次幫狗洗澡都覺得自己在表演諜對諜的朋友——他剛剛又被噴了一身水
variations: 進階版：你以為洗澡是日常護理。對牠來說，那是定期上演的恐怖片。
備註：吹完之後那十分鐘的瘋狂打滾，是牠用全身告訴你：好了，我原諒你了，下次還是不要。

en.content:
The dog, before bath time:

hears the word "bath" and vanishes. You eventually find it under the couch, eyes accusing you of betraying the revolution.

The dog, during bath time:

drenched, fur flattened, mass reduced by half, looking like life has been wrung out of its body. Eyes filled with the despair of a creature whose glorious life is being personally ended by you.

The dog, after bath time:

blow-dried into a fluffy cloud, rolling on the carpet like it's possessed, doing eight victory laps around the couch, rubbing its butt against your leg. Joy levels of someone who has just been released from prison.

Same dog. Same hour. Three completely different lifetimes.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose every bath day feels like a covert operation — they just got sprayed in the face again
variations: Advanced: you think a bath is routine care. To the dog, it's a horror film that gets re-released on a regular schedule.
Footnote: those ten minutes of post-bath zoomies are your dog's full-body announcement: okay, I forgive you, but let's not do this again.

items.sourceNote: 養狗才懂的家規矛盾，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
家規第一條：狗狗不可以上沙發。

現況：牠正躺在沙發正中間，四腳朝天，打呼。

你坐沙發邊緣，怕吵到牠。
你拿薄毯蓋牠，怕牠冷。
你滑手機都不敢出聲，怕打擾牠午睡。

所以家規到底是誰寫的？
誰在執行？
誰才是這個家真正的甲方？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個家裡「明明說好不上沙發、結果沙發整張變成牠的床」的朋友——他現在大概正蹲在地上
variations: 進階觀察：家規不是失效，是早就被牠用一個翻肚的動作正式廢除了。
備註：你買了狗床、狗墊、狗的專屬區域，最後牠選擇睡在你的腿上。錢花得很值得，因為你終於確認了——牠選的是你，不是床。

en.content:
House rule #1: the dog is not allowed on the couch.

Current situation: it is lying in the exact center of the couch, all four paws in the air, snoring.

You perch on the edge so you don't disturb it.
You drape a thin blanket over it in case it's cold.
You scroll your phone in silence so its nap isn't interrupted.

So who exactly wrote the house rules?
Who enforces them?
Who is the real authority in this household?
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose 'no dogs on the couch' policy ended with the entire couch becoming the dog's bed — they're probably sitting on the floor right now
variations: Advanced observation: the rule didn't fail. It was formally repealed the moment the dog rolled onto its back.
Footnote: you bought the dog bed, the dog mat, the dedicated dog corner. The dog chose your lap. Money well spent — you confirmed the dog picked you, not the bed.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對「不在家」的時間感知，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你上班八小時，對你來說是「今天很長」。
對牠來說，是「我以為你不會回來了」。

你開門那一刻，
牠的反應不是「你回來了」，
是「你居然真的回來了」。

那種驚喜、感動、難以置信，
你在人類社會裡，大概一輩子也得不到一次。

但每天下班，牠都給你一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給任何一個「下班開門前會稍微停一下、聽裡面腳步聲」的朋友——他其實知道自己被誰等
variations: 進階版：你以為你在養牠。其實，是牠每天用八小時的想念，溫柔地養著你的存在感。
備註：人這輩子很難被誰這樣等。所以那扇門一開，請你蹲下來抱抱牠，那是雙向奔赴。

en.content:
You worked eight hours. For you, that was a long day.
For your dog, that was "I genuinely thought you weren't coming back."

The moment you open the door,
its reaction isn't "oh, you're home,"
it's "you actually came back."

That level of surprise, gratitude, and disbelief —
in the human world, you might never get it once in a lifetime.

Your dog gives it to you every single day after work.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who pauses outside their door after work just to hear the footsteps inside — they already know who's waiting
variations: Advanced version: you think you're raising the dog. Actually, the dog is using eight hours of missing you every day to quietly remind you that you matter.
Footnote: very few people in your life will ever wait for you like that. So when the door opens, kneel down and hug it back. That's a two-way street.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗食物雷達的精準度，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在房間裡，牠在客廳睡覺。

你拆開一包餅乾。

零點三秒後，
牠出現在你腳邊，眼神純真、尾巴輕搖、
好像那個午睡的不是牠，是另一隻同款狗。

科學家研究黑洞、宇宙、量子糾纏，
但沒人能解釋——
為什麼狗狗的耳朵，可以選擇性失靈地不聽你叫牠回家，
卻能在三層牆外，聽見起司包裝紙的聲音。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個「拆零食必須躲到廁所、結果狗還是會出現在門口」的朋友——他笑完會嘆氣承認
variations: 進階版：你叫牠的名字，牠裝聾。你打開冰箱，牠瞬間瞬移。這不是聽力問題，是優先順序問題。
備註：牠不是貪吃。牠只是想確認，你吃的東西，有沒有牠的份。確認完，牠會帶著失望離開——然後三分鐘後再回來確認一次。

en.content:
You're in the bedroom. The dog is asleep in the living room.

You open a bag of crackers.

Zero point three seconds later,
it's at your feet, eyes innocent, tail gently wagging,
as if the dog that was napping a moment ago was a completely different but identical-looking dog.

Scientists study black holes, the universe, quantum entanglement,
but no one can explain
why a dog's ears can selectively malfunction when you call it home,
yet still pick up the sound of a cheese wrapper through three walls.
en.editorNote: Send to every friend who has to hide in the bathroom to eat a snack — the dog still shows up at the door, every single time
variations: Advanced version: you call its name, suddenly it's deaf. You open the fridge, suddenly it teleports. This isn't a hearing problem. It's a priority problem.

Footnote: it's not greedy. It just needs to confirm that whatever you're eating, there's a share for it. After confirming, it'll walk away disappointed — and come back three minutes later to confirm again.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對情緒的偵測能力，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你今天工作上被罵、會議搞砸、回家路上下雨。

你開門，沒有力氣笑。

但牠一看到你，停了。
沒有平常的衝過來、沒有甩尾巴、沒有討抱。

牠只是慢慢走過來，把頭靠在你的腿上，
然後一聲不吭，陪你站在玄關。

你不知道牠怎麼知道的。
但牠就是知道。

那一刻你才懂——
有些理解，根本不需要語言。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給今天過得不太好的朋友——告訴他：家裡那隻，已經偵測到了，等你回家
variations: 進階版：人類花一輩子學「同理心」，狗狗用鼻子三秒鐘就完成。
備註：牠不會問你「你還好嗎」，因為牠根本不需要你回答。牠只是用整個身體告訴你：我在。

en.content:
You had a rough day at work. Got chewed out, blew a meeting, and it rained on the way home.

You open the door without the energy to smile.

But the moment your dog sees you, it pauses.
No charging over, no tail wagging, no demanding a hug.

It just walks over slowly, rests its head on your leg,
and stands there with you in the entryway, completely silent.

You don't know how it knew.
But it just knew.

That's when you realize —
some forms of understanding don't need words at all.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's having a hard day — tell them: the one at home already sensed it, and it's waiting
variations: Advanced version: humans spend a lifetime trying to learn empathy. Dogs do it in three seconds, with their nose.
Footnote: it won't ask 'are you okay,' because it doesn't need an answer. It uses its whole body to say one thing: I'm here.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗突然爆衝跑zoomies的場景觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨十一點半，你已經躺在床上，準備睡覺。

剛剛還趴在地毯上打呼的那隻生物，突然睜眼。
牠站起來，原地看了三秒鐘。
然後——

屁股一夾，從客廳衝到房間。
從房間衝到廚房。
從廚房滑過你剛拖好的地板。
中間還繞著沙發跑兩圈。

你看著牠，毫無頭緒。
牠也沒有要解釋的意思。

這不是運動，這是狗狗每天的隨機事件。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次半夜被狗狗突然暴衝吵醒的朋友——他完全知道那個從零加速到一百的瞬間
variations: 進階版：跑完之後，牠會立刻倒在你腳邊睡著，好像剛剛那段三十秒的瘋狂，從來沒有發生過。
附帶觀察：你看獸醫，獸醫說「這是健康的表現」。你笑著點頭，但內心覺得，這個「健康」也太有戲劇張力了。

en.content:
It's 11:30 PM. You're already in bed, ready to sleep.

The creature that was snoring on the rug two minutes ago suddenly opens its eyes.
It stands up. Stares at nothing for three seconds.
Then —

butt tucks in, full sprint from the living room to the bedroom.
Bedroom to the kitchen.
Slides across the floor you just mopped.
Laps the couch twice for good measure.

You stare at it, completely lost.
It offers no explanation.

This isn't exercise. This is a random event your dog rolls for, daily.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who's been jolted awake by midnight zoomies — they know that zero-to-one-hundred ignition exactly
variations: Advanced version: as soon as the sprint ends, it collapses at your feet and falls asleep, as if those wild thirty seconds never happened.
Bonus: you ask the vet, the vet says 'that's a sign of a healthy dog.' You nod politely, while privately thinking — healthy comes with a lot more drama than expected.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡覺做夢抽搐汪汪的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗狗趴在地上睡得很熟。

突然，牠的腳開始抽動。
抽動三下、停一下。
抽動三下、停一下。
然後嘴巴小聲地「汪、汪、汪」。

你看著牠，認真地問自己：

牠夢到自己在追松鼠嗎？
夢到被追嗎？
夢到我又忘記買零食了嗎？

你永遠不會知道。
但你會默默把手機拿出來，錄下來。
因為這個畫面，是你今天最幸福的兩分鐘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都被狗狗睡夢中軟軟的「汪」融化的朋友——他的相簿裡至少有五段這種影片
variations: 進階版：你輕輕摸牠一下，牠停止抽動，深吸一口氣，繼續睡。你像是不小心打斷了一部牠正在主演的動作片。
附帶觀察：牠睡夢中那一聲「汪」的音量，永遠剛好不會吵醒自己，但會被你錄到三十段不同版本。

en.content:
The dog is sound asleep on the floor.

Suddenly, its legs start twitching.
Three twitches, pause.
Three twitches, pause.
Then its mouth lets out a quiet "woof, woof, woof."

You watch and genuinely ask yourself:

Is it dreaming about chasing a squirrel?
Dreaming about being chased?
Dreaming that I forgot the treats again?

You'll never know.
But you quietly pull out your phone and start recording.
Because this is the happiest two minutes of your day.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who melts at the tiny dream-woofs — their camera roll already has at least five clips of this
variations: Advanced version: you gently touch the dog. It stops twitching, takes one deep breath, and keeps sleeping. You feel like you just paused an action movie it was starring in.
Bonus: those dream-woofs are always perfectly quiet enough not to wake the dog itself, and perfectly loud enough for you to capture in thirty different versions.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗選擇性聽力—名字無感、零食袋秒到的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你叫牠的名字三次。
第一次：耳朵動一下。
第二次：眼睛微張，但沒有要起來的意思。
第三次：直接把頭埋進前腳，假裝聽不到。

你嘆口氣，走去廚房。
你還沒打開零食櫃，只是輕輕把袋子捏了一下——

沙——一聲。

下一秒，牠已經坐在你腳邊。
眼神乾淨、姿勢標準、彷彿剛剛那個叫不動的不是牠。

狗狗不是聽不見。
牠只是有自己的優先順序系統。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都被自家狗狗用「選擇性聽力」打敗的朋友——他知道零食袋的聲音比名字有用一百倍
variations: 進階版：你想測試牠，故意捏一下空袋子。牠衝過來、發現是空的之後，回頭看你的眼神——是真正的失望。
附帶觀察：你叫名字是請求，零食袋是聖旨。順序從你養牠的第三週開始，就已經定下來了。

en.content:
You call its name three times.
First time: one ear twitches.
Second time: eyes crack open, no intention of getting up.
Third time: head buried into its paws, pretending not to hear.

You sigh and walk to the kitchen.
You haven't even opened the treat cabinet — you just lightly crinkle the bag —

crinkle.

Next second, it's sitting at your feet.
Clean eyes, perfect posture, as if the dog who ignored you three seconds ago was a completely different animal.

Dogs aren't deaf.
They just have their own priority system.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who loses every round of 'selective hearing' at home — they know the treat bag outranks the dog's own name by a factor of one hundred
variations: Advanced version: you test it by crinkling an empty bag. It runs over, realizes it's empty, then turns and looks at you with genuine disappointment.
Bonus: calling the name is a request. The treat bag is a royal decree. The order was settled by week three of you owning this dog.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對新交往對象的審核，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你帶新對象第一次回家。

你介紹完房間、介紹完家人、最後介紹你家狗狗。

狗狗的反應，會決定這段感情的走向。

情境一：尾巴搖到要飛起來，撲上去舔對方的臉。
→ 你心裡鬆一口氣，這個人通過了。

情境二：聞一下，淡淡地走開，去窗邊趴下。
→ 你開始默默觀察對方有沒有什麼地方怪怪的。

情境三：發出低吼，退後三步，把自己擋在你前面。
→ 你心裡已經有答案，雖然你嘴上還在打圓場。

人類看履歷，狗狗看靈魂。
後者的命中率，從來沒讓你失望。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次交新對象都要先過「狗狗審核」這關的朋友——他相信狗狗的直覺，更勝過自己的判斷
variations: 進階版：朋友問你「你怎麼判斷一個人能不能交往」，你正色回答：「我家狗狗已經幫我看過了。」朋友點頭，覺得這個說法意外地合理。
附帶觀察：你跟人分手那一刻，狗狗會走過來，把頭靠在你腿上。牠沒有說「我早就告訴你了」——但你知道，牠早就知道了。

en.content:
You bring someone new home for the first time.

You show them the apartment, introduce your family, and finally introduce your dog.

The dog's reaction will quietly decide the entire future of this relationship.

Scenario A: tail wagging hard enough to lift off, full body launch, licks the person's face.
→ You exhale. They passed.

Scenario B: one sniff, casual walk away, settles by the window.
→ You start quietly noticing things you missed.

Scenario C: low growl, steps backward, plants itself between you and them.
→ You already know how this ends, even while you're still being polite.

Humans read résumés. Dogs read souls.
The second one has a much better hit rate.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose dating life passes through a dog-approval checkpoint — they trust the dog's instincts more than their own
variations: Advanced version: a friend asks how you decide if someone is dateable. You answer seriously: 'my dog already screened them.' The friend nods, finding this surprisingly reasonable.
Bonus: the moment a breakup happens, the dog walks over and rests its head on your leg. It doesn't say 'I told you so' — but you know it knew, long before you did.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛變成日常穿搭配件的接受，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出門前對著鏡子確認服裝。

上衣：乾淨。
褲子：乾淨。
領子上：一根白色狗毛。
袖口上：一根黑色狗毛。
膝蓋附近：一團不知道哪裡來的狗毛。

你嘆口氣，拿起黏毛滾筒。
滾完左邊滾右邊，滾完前面滾後面。

你走出門，到公司打開外套——
領子上又有一根狗毛。

你已經不生氣了。
你接受了：那不是衣服上的瑕疵，那是你身上「有人愛你」的證明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天出門前都要跟狗毛打仗、最後總是放棄的朋友——他知道那根狗毛，就是他這個身分的證件照
variations: 進階版：你開始買跟你家狗狗顏色一樣的衣服。這不是時尚，這是工程學。
附帶觀察：你去朋友家，朋友家也有養狗。回家之後，你身上有兩個品牌的狗毛。你笑了一下，覺得這是養狗人之間的祕密握手。

en.content:
You check your outfit in the mirror before heading out.

Shirt: clean.
Pants: clean.
Collar: one white dog hair.
Cuff: one black dog hair.
Knee area: a tuft of dog hair, source unknown.

You sigh and pick up the lint roller.
Left side, right side. Front, back.

You walk into the office, take off your coat —
there's another dog hair on the collar.

You're not even annoyed anymore.
You've accepted it: that's not a flaw on your clothes. That's proof, in writing, that someone at home loves you.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who fights the dog-hair war every morning and surrenders by lunch — that single hair on the collar is basically their ID badge now
variations: Advanced version: you start buying clothes in the same color as your dog. This isn't fashion. This is engineering.
Bonus: you visit a friend who also has a dog. You go home wearing two different brands of dog hair. You smile — that's the secret handshake of dog people.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看醫生前一秒識破主人計畫的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你早上起床，刻意保持非常自然的表情。
你用平常的語氣說：「走，我們散步。」
你拿牽繩、穿鞋子、開門——所有動作都跟平常一模一樣。

但牠走到門口，突然停下。
抬頭看你。
眼神告訴你：「今天不是散步，對吧？」

你還沒講出口，牠已經知道了。
你已經不是在帶一隻狗去看醫生，
你是在跟一個比你還早讀完劇本的偵探鬥智。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次帶狗去看醫生都覺得「牠怎麼又識破我了」的朋友——他已經放棄演技這條路了
variations: 進階版：你開車的時候，前三個紅綠燈牠都很正常。第四個紅綠燈一轉彎，牠突然在後座發出一聲長嘆。那條路，是醫院的方向。
附加觀察：看完醫生回家，牠會故意繞過你五分鐘，提醒你今天這件事已經被記在小本本上。

en.content:
You wake up and put on your most natural face.
You say, in your normal voice: "Hey, let's go for a walk."
You grab the leash, put on your shoes, open the door — every motion identical to a regular morning.

But the dog walks to the entrance and stops.
Looks up at you.
Eyes saying: "Today isn't a walk, is it?"

You haven't even said the word.
It already knows.
You are no longer taking a dog to the vet.
You are outmatched by a detective who read the script before you did.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose vet-day cover story keeps getting blown — they've already given up on their acting career
variations: Advanced version: in the car, the first three traffic lights go fine. At the fourth turn, the dog lets out one long sigh from the back seat. That road leads to the vet.
Bonus: after the appointment, the dog avoids you for five minutes on purpose — just so you know today's incident has been formally recorded.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對家中新家具的接收儀式觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了三個禮拜的薪水，買了一張新沙發。
師傅扛進門，你連塑膠膜都還沒拆。

但牠已經跳上去了。

牠在沙發上轉三圈，趴下，閉眼。
那不是試躺，那是一份正式的所有權公告：
「這張沙發，現在屬於我。你可以坐角落。」

師傅看了一眼，看了你一眼，露出全宇宙都懂的微笑。
你也只能微笑。

那張沙發，從進門那一刻，就不是你的了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次買新家具都被狗狗「先住進去」的朋友——他知道家具是租給狗狗用的
variations: 進階版：你嘗試在牠不在的時候搶回中間的位置。三秒後牠出現，用整顆頭頂著你的腿，慢慢地、堅定地把你擠到邊邊。
附帶觀察：你買的時候挑了好幾個小時的顏色、材質、深度。牠完全沒有意見，因為牠知道——反正最後都是牠在睡。

en.content:
You spent three weeks of salary on a new couch.
The delivery guy hauls it in. You haven't even removed the plastic wrap.

The dog is already on it.

It circles three times, lies down, closes its eyes.
That's not a test sit. That's a formal declaration of ownership:
"This couch is mine now. You may sit in the corner."

The delivery guy glances at the dog, then at you, and smiles the universal smile.
You smile back.

The couch stopped being yours the moment it crossed the threshold.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose new furniture gets claimed by the dog within sixty seconds — they understand the lease arrangement
variations: Advanced version: you try to reclaim the middle seat while the dog is away. Three seconds later it returns, leans the full weight of its head against your thigh, and slowly, deliberately, pushes you back to the edge.
Bonus: you spent hours choosing the color, fabric, and seat depth. The dog had no opinion on any of it — because the dog already knew it would be the one sleeping there.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗記得味道、記得人的長期觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你有一件衣服，平常你不太穿。
是兩年前，一個離開的朋友送你的。

你拿出來想丟掉，攤在床上。

牠走過來，聞了聞，趴上去，眼睛瞇起來。

你愣住了。
那個味道，那個人，那個你以為自己已經放下的故事——
牠都記得。

你坐下來，沒有把衣服收走。
那一刻你才知道：
你身邊那隻狗，比你更認真地記得你愛過誰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近在整理舊東西、家裡有狗的朋友——這段話會幫他理解，原來有些事情並沒有真的消失
variations: 延伸版：你以為自己是用人類的方式記住一個人——回憶、照片、對話。其實牠是用最古老的方式記得：味道、體溫、那個人摸過牠的手。
附加觀察：你那天沒有丟那件衣服。你把它疊好，放回衣櫃最裡面。原來放下，不一定是丟掉。

en.content:
There's a piece of clothing you barely wear.
A friend who left your life gave it to you, two years ago.

You pull it out, planning to throw it away. You lay it on the bed.

The dog walks over. Sniffs. Climbs on top of it. Closes its eyes halfway.

You freeze.
That scent, that person, the whole story you thought you'd let go of —
the dog remembers all of it.

You sit down. You don't put the clothing away.
In that moment, you realize:
the dog next to you remembers who you've loved more carefully than you do.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend sorting through old things who has a dog at home — this will help them understand that some things didn't actually disappear
variations: Extended: you thought you remembered people the human way — memories, photos, conversations. The dog remembers in the oldest way there is: scent, warmth, the hand that once touched its fur.
Bonus: you didn't throw the clothing out that day. You folded it and tucked it into the back of the closet. Letting go, it turns out, doesn't always mean throwing away.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對特定家人的差別待遇觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你每天負責：餵牠、帶牠散步、洗澡、剪指甲、處理嘔吐、半夜被牠用爪子拍醒。

你媽偶爾來一趟：摸牠三下、餵牠一塊雞肉。

結果你媽進門那一刻，牠尾巴搖到快斷掉。
你下班回家，牠抬個頭，眼神告訴你：「喔，是你。」

你終於明白：
你是員工，你媽是 VIP。
你負責營運，你媽負責收割。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡狗狗對長輩特別熱情、對自己很冷淡的朋友——這個世界對毛主人特別不公平
variations: 進階版：你出差三天回來，牠尾巴搖一下就走了。你媽出去買個菜回來，牠瘋狂繞圈尖叫。這份差別待遇沒有任何解釋，你只能接受。
附帶觀察：你跟你媽都在的時候，牠永遠先靠在你媽旁邊。你只能在心裡安慰自己：至少牠半夜踢被子會踢到我，這代表牠潛意識相信我。

en.content:
Daily, you handle: feeding, walks, baths, nail trims, cleaning up vomit, getting woken at 3am by a small paw to the face.

Your mom visits occasionally: pets the dog three times, hands over one piece of chicken.

The second your mom walks in, the dog's tail spins so hard it might fall off.
You come home from work — the dog lifts its head and gives you a look that says, "Oh. It's you."

You finally understand:
you are the staff. Your mom is the VIP.
You run operations. She harvests the joy.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog rolls out the red carpet for visiting relatives but greets them with mild indifference — the universe is unfair to primary caregivers
variations: Advanced version: you come back from a three-day work trip — one tail wag, then it walks away. Your mom returns from the grocery store — full spinning, full screaming. There is no explanation. You just accept.

Bonus: when you and your mom are both in the room, the dog always picks her side. You comfort yourself with this: at night, the dog kicks the blanket onto you. That means subconsciously, it trusts you.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗夢境觀察、抽搐叫聲的溫柔解讀，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，牠在你腳邊睡著。

突然，牠的腿開始抽動。
嘴巴發出一個小小的「嗚」。
尾巴輕輕拍了兩下棉被。

牠在做夢。

你坐起來看牠，心裡忍不住想：
牠夢到什麼？
是夢到追松鼠？是夢到飛起來？
還是——夢到你？

你後來相信是最後一個。
因為一個會在夢裡搖尾巴的生物，
夢裡那個畫面，一定是牠最喜歡的東西。

而牠最喜歡的東西，從來就只是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有狗、會默默在半夜看牠睡覺的朋友——這段話會把那個畫面的意義講出來
variations: 延伸版：你輕輕摸了一下牠的頭，牠在夢裡嘆了一口氣，把臉貼得更靠近你的腳。原來連在夢裡，牠都知道你在這裡。
附加版：科學家說狗狗會夢到主人。其實不用科學家說，你看牠尾巴搖的方向，你就知道夢的另一邊是誰。

en.content:
It's 3 a.m. The dog is asleep by your feet.

Suddenly, its legs start twitching.
A tiny "mmf" escapes its mouth.
The tail gives the blanket two soft thumps.

It's dreaming.

You sit up and watch, and you can't help wondering:
What is it dreaming about?
Chasing squirrels? Flying? Or —
dreaming of you?

You decide to believe it's the last one.
Because a creature that wags its tail inside a dream
is dreaming about its favorite thing in the world.

And its favorite thing has always been you.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who quietly watches their dog sleep at night — this puts words on what that moment actually is
variations: Extended: you gently rest a hand on its head. It sighs inside the dream and presses its face closer to your foot. Even in sleep, it knew you were there.
Bonus: scientists say dogs dream about their owners. You don't really need the scientists. Just watch which direction the tail is wagging — you already know who's on the other side of the dream.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗監督人類做飯的日常，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗狗有一份隱形的工作。

職稱：零食監督官。

上班時間：你只要走進廚房，牠就立刻就定位。
薪水：地上掉下來的任何東西。
工作內容：盯著你的每一個動作，從開冰箱到拆包裝，全程不眨眼。

牠不需要會做菜，
牠只負責確保——
沒有任何一塊食物，能在無人監督的情況下進到你的嘴裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個煮飯時腳邊永遠卡著一隻狗的朋友
variations: 精簡版：我家狗狗的人生目標很單純：確保我吃的每一口，牠都有先審核過。
延伸版：你以為你是在自己家做飯，其實你是在牠的監督下進行一場食物分配聽證會。

en.content:
My dog has an invisible job.

Title: Supervisor of Snacks.

Hours: the second you walk into the kitchen, it clocks in.
Salary: anything that hits the floor.
Duties: track your every move, from opening the fridge to tearing the wrapper, without a single blink.

It doesn't need to know how to cook.
It's only here to make sure
not one piece of food reaches your mouth unsupervised.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always has a dog wedged against their ankles while cooking
variations: Short version: my dog's life goal is simple — to personally pre-approve every bite I take.
Bonus: you think you're cooking in your own kitchen. Actually you're running a food-distribution hearing under its supervision.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗不懂個人空間只懂關著的門，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗狗的字典裡，沒有「個人空間」這四個字。

牠只認得一種東西：

關著的門。

你進廁所，門關上——牠在外面用鼻子頂。
你進房間想靜一靜，門關上——牠把下巴壓在門縫底下嘆氣。
你只是想單獨待五分鐘——這在牠看來，是一種需要立刻被糾正的錯誤。

在牠的世界觀裡：
任何一扇關起來的門，
都是一個還沒被解決的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個上廁所都有狗在門外等的人
variations: 精簡版：狗狗不懂什麼叫做想自己靜一靜，牠只懂那扇門為什麼要對牠關起來。
延伸版：你以為你關門是為了隱私，牠以為你關門是不小心的，所以牠很貼心地，在外面等你發現這個錯誤。

en.content:
There's no such phrase as "personal space" in a dog's dictionary.

It only recognizes one thing:

a closed door.

You go to the bathroom, door shuts — it noses at the gap from outside.
You step into a room for some quiet, door shuts — it presses its chin under the crack and sighs.
You just want five minutes alone — and to the dog, that's an error that must be corrected immediately.

In its worldview,
every closed door
is a problem that hasn't been solved yet.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who has an audience outside the bathroom door
variations: Short version: a dog doesn't understand wanting to be alone. It only understands why that door is closed against it.
Bonus: you close the door for privacy. It assumes you closed it by accident — so it kindly waits outside for you to notice the mistake.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對你眨眼像在傳暗號，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗狗有時候會對我眨一下眼。

我一定眨回去。

以防萬一——
那是一個暗號。

萬一牠其實是某個祕密組織派來的，
萬一那一眨，是在說「行動開始」，
萬一我沒回，牠就會以為我臨陣脫逃。

所以我每次都認真眨回去。

兩個搞不清楚狀況的傢伙，
在客廳裡，用眼神交換著一個誰都不知道內容的祕密。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個會跟自家狗對眼神的人——你們之間一定也有只有你們懂的默契
variations: 精簡版：狗狗對我眨眼，我就眨回去。萬一是暗號呢？我可不想錯過什麼重要任務。
延伸版：後來我發現牠只是眼睛癢。但沒關係，我們的祕密組織還是繼續運作，會員人數：兩名。

en.content:
Sometimes my dog winks at me.

I always wink back.

Just in case —
it's a code.

In case it's actually an agent from some secret organization,
in case that wink means "the mission is a go,"
in case I don't respond and it thinks I bailed at the last second.

So I wink back, every time, very seriously.

Two clueless creatures,
in the living room,
trading a secret that neither of us knows the contents of.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who locks eyes with their dog — you two definitely have your own private code
variations: Short version: my dog winks, I wink back. What if it's a signal? I'm not about to miss an important mission.
Bonus: turns out its eye was just itchy. No matter — our secret organization is still up and running. Members: two.

items.sourceNote: 養狗後居家風格變狗毛主題，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家不是亂，
是專門為狗設計的。

沙發顏色：原本是深藍，現在是「深藍加一層薄薄的毛」。
地板裝飾：散落各處的玩具，擺放方式只有牠懂。
空氣主題：一打開窗，陽光照進來，你會看見毛在光裡優雅地飛。

朋友來我家，說：「你家好像有養狗喔。」
我說：「對，這是我們的設計理念。」

沒有任何居家風格，
是缺了狗毛還能算完整的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個放棄跟狗毛對抗、決定跟它和平共處的人
variations: 精簡版：我家不是髒，是用狗毛重新定義過的極簡風——簡單到連黑色衣服都自帶裝飾。
延伸版：我已經不滾黏毛滾筒了。我把那層毛當成室內設計的一部分，免費的、會自己更新的、軟綿綿的牆面材質。

en.content:
My house isn't messy.

It's custom designed for dogs.

Sofa color: was navy, now it's "navy with a thin top coat of fur."
Floor décor: toys scattered everywhere, arranged in a layout only the dog understands.
Air theme: open a window, let the sun in, and watch the fur drift elegantly through the light.

A friend comes over and says, "Looks like you've got a dog."
I say, "Yes. That's the design concept."

No home décor
is ever truly complete
without a layer of dog hair.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who gave up fighting the fur and decided to live in peace with it
variations: Short version: my place isn't dirty, it's minimalism redefined by dog hair — so minimal even my black clothes come pre-accessorized.
Bonus: I stopped using the lint roller. I treat the fur as part of the interior design — free, self-renewing, soft wall texture.

items.sourceNote: 洗澡時狗狗覺得被背叛的梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
前一秒，牠還是世界上最愛你的生物。

後一秒，你把牠抱進浴室。

那個眼神瞬間變了——
從「你是我的全世界」
變成「我們之間，結束了」。

牠用濕答答的眼睛看你，
好像在說：我以為你跟別人不一樣。

洗澡，是狗狗確認「人類終究會背叛我」的時刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次幫狗洗澡都要先做心理建設的人——他懂那種被當成叛徒的委屈
variations: 精簡版：狗狗對你的信任，可以維持一輩子。除非你打開蓮蓬頭。
延伸版：洗完牠會用全身甩水報復你，然後在地上瘋狂打滾，努力把「狗味」找回來。畢竟那是牠的尊嚴。

en.content:
One second ago, your dog was the creature that loved you most in the world.

The next second, you carried it into the bathroom.

And the look changed instantly —
from "you are my whole world"
to "we are done here."

It stares at you with those wet, betrayed eyes,
as if to say: I thought you were different.

Bath time is the moment a dog confirms that humans will, in the end, betray you.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who needs a pep talk before bathing their dog — they know that look of pure betrayal
variations: Short version: your dog's trust can last a lifetime. Unless you turn on the showerhead.
Bonus: afterward it shakes water all over you for revenge, then rolls on the floor like crazy trying to get its 'dog smell' back. That's its dignity, after all.

items.sourceNote: 想成為狗狗眼中那個好人，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子最大的目標，
就是努力變成一個——
跟我家狗以為的我一樣好的人。

牠覺得我聰明、勇敢、無所不能，
覺得我每天都會準時回家，
覺得我手上拿的零食永遠分得到牠。

牠對我的信任，
高到有點不切實際。

但也正因為這樣，
我才想活成那個值得牠信任的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個因為養了狗、默默想變成更好的人的朋友
variations: 精簡版：我家狗以為我是好人，所以我只好努力不讓牠失望。
延伸版：在牠眼裡我永遠是英雄。我沒辦法戳破這個美好的誤會，只好真的去當那個英雄。

en.content:
My biggest goal in life
is to become the kind of person
my dog already thinks I am.

It thinks I'm smart, brave, and capable of anything.
It thinks I always make it home on time.
It thinks whatever snack is in my hand will somehow be shared.

Its trust in me
is honestly a little unrealistic.

But that's exactly why
I want to live up to being someone worthy of it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who quietly became a better person after getting a dog
variations: Short version: my dog thinks I'm a good person, so now I have to try not to let it down.
Bonus: I'm a hero in its eyes, every single day. I can't bring myself to ruin that beautiful misunderstanding, so I just go ahead and be the hero.

items.sourceNote: 外星人看人類撿狗大便的梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果外星人來地球，
看到我們牽著狗散步，
然後彎下腰、用塑膠袋撿起牠的大便——

你覺得，
他們會以為誰才是主人？

狗狗走在前面，昂首闊步。
人類跟在後面，提著一袋溫熱的成果。

我們一直以為自己在養狗，
但從某個角度看，
好像也說不太清楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天乖乖牽狗、彎腰撿便的鏟屎官——這就是你的日常寫照
variations: 精簡版：到底誰在養誰？我提著大便袋的時候，常常會認真思考這個問題。
延伸版：牠負責散步，我負責後勤。這個分工，到底是誰決定的？反正不是我。

en.content:
If aliens landed on Earth
and watched us walking our dogs,
then bending down to scoop up their poop with a little plastic bag —

who do you think
they'd assume is in charge?

The dog struts ahead, head held high.
The human trails behind, holding a warm little bag of results.

We always thought we were the ones who owned the dog,
but from a certain angle,
it's honestly hard to say.
en.editorNote: Send to the dutiful poop-scooper who walks their dog every day — this is literally your life
variations: Short version: who's really walking whom? I think about this a lot while holding a warm bag of poop.
Bonus: the dog handles the walking, I handle logistics. Who decided on this division of labor? Not me, that's for sure.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗不在意你的身分地位只要你的心，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗狗不在乎你有沒有錢，
不在乎你書讀得多不多，
不在乎你今天表現好不好、有沒有被罵。

牠不會因為你失業就少愛你一點，
也不會因為你成功就多巴結你幾分。

你只要把心交給牠，
牠就把整顆心還給你——
而且不留一點保留，也不算利息。

這世界上，
很少有什麼愛，是這麼乾淨的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得自己不夠好、需要被無條件接住的人
variations: 精簡版：狗狗不問你是誰，只在乎你回家了沒。
延伸版：人會看你的頭銜、看你的存款、看你今天有沒有用。只有牠，只看你有沒有回來。

en.content:
Your dog doesn't care whether you're rich,
doesn't care how many books you've read,
doesn't care if today went well or if you got chewed out at work.

It won't love you less because you lost your job,
and it won't suck up to you more because you succeeded.

Give it your heart,
and it gives its whole heart back —
no part held in reserve, no interest charged.

In this world,
very little love is ever that clean.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone feeling not-good-enough who needs to be caught unconditionally
variations: Short version: your dog doesn't ask who you are. It only cares whether you came home.
Bonus: people look at your title, your savings, whether you were useful today. Only your dog just looks at whether you came back.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗社交生活比主人還精彩的梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我認真覺得，
我家狗的社交生活比我精彩。

出門散步十分鐘，
牠跟三隻狗打過招呼、
被兩個路人摸頭稱讚、
還跟巷口那隻貓眼神交流了一下。

而我，
站在旁邊，
滑著一個沒人回覆的群組。

所以我們才這麼合得來——
牠負責有人緣，
我負責當牠的經紀人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個假日唯一的社交就是陪狗散步的朋友——他會笑著承認
variations: 精簡版：我家狗的朋友比我多，我已經接受了這個事實，並決定當牠的隨行助理。
延伸版：散步時所有人都在跟牠打招呼，沒人理我。我終於體會到什麼叫「名人的家屬」。

en.content:
I'm genuinely convinced
my dog has a better social life than I do.

Ten minutes into a walk,
it's greeted three other dogs,
gotten head pats and compliments from two strangers,
and exchanged a meaningful glance with the cat at the corner.

Meanwhile I'm just
standing there,
scrolling a group chat nobody replied to.

That's exactly why we get along so well —
it handles being popular,
and I handle being its manager.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose only weekend socializing is walking the dog — they'll laugh and admit it
variations: Short version: my dog has more friends than I do. I've accepted it and decided to be its personal assistant.
Bonus: on walks, everyone says hi to the dog and nobody acknowledges me. So this is what it's like to be a celebrity's family member.

items.sourceNote: 老狗陪伴漸漸變慢的時光，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠的腳步慢了，
上樓梯要停一下，
聽你叫牠的時候，要多花一秒才轉頭。

但牠看你的眼神沒變，
還是那個第一天就決定要愛你一輩子的眼神。

我們總以為陪伴是牠在等我們，
後來才懂，
是我們一直在偷偷數，還剩多少時間能陪牠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有老狗的朋友——不用多說，他會懂
variations: 精簡版：牠老了，走得慢了，但愛你的速度從來沒慢過。
延伸版：以前是牠追著你跑，現在換你放慢腳步，配合牠的節奏。這不是退讓，是輪到你照顧牠了。

en.content:
Its steps have slowed.
It pauses halfway up the stairs,
and takes an extra second to turn when you call its name.

But the way it looks at you hasn't changed —
still the same look it gave you on day one,
the day it decided to love you for the rest of its life.

We always thought companionship meant it waiting for us.
Later you realize
we were the ones quietly counting how much time we had left with it.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with an aging dog — no explanation needed, they'll understand
variations: Short version: it's old now, it walks slower, but it has never loved you any slower.
Bonus: it used to chase after you. Now you slow your steps to match its pace. That's not giving in — it's your turn to take care of it.

items.sourceNote: 領養米克斯救援狗的雙向救贖，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
領養那天，
你以為是你救了牠。

你帶牠離開冰冷的籠子，
給牠一個有名字、有飯吃、有床睡的家。

但住久了你才發現，
那個下班回家有人等、
半夜難過有人靠過來、
再爛的一天都還有理由起床的人，

其實是你。

到底是誰救了誰，
早就分不清了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給正在考慮領養、或剛領養回家的朋友
variations: 精簡版：我以為我領養了一隻狗，後來發現是牠收養了一個需要被愛的我。
延伸版：牠不知道自己曾經差點沒有明天。牠只知道，現在每一天都有你。

en.content:
On adoption day,
you thought you were the one doing the rescuing.

You carried it out of a cold cage
and gave it a home with a name, a bowl, a bed.

But after a while you realized
the one who now has someone waiting after work,
someone leaning in on the rough nights,
a reason to get up even on the worst days —

is you.

Who rescued whom
stopped being clear a long time ago.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend thinking about adopting, or one who just brought a rescue home
variations: Short version: I thought I adopted a dog. Turns out it adopted a version of me that needed loving.
Bonus: it doesn't know it almost didn't have a tomorrow. It just knows that now, every day has you in it.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗把破壞當才藝表演的幽默，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗有一個神奇的能力：

只要我心情好、家裡很乾淨、那天過得特別順，
牠就會精準地，在這個時刻，
把整捲衛生紙拆成一場雪景。

然後牠會站在現場，
尾巴搖得超用力，
眼神寫著：「看！我幫你重新佈置了！」

你根本氣不起來，
因為牠是真心覺得自己做了一件好事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個家裡永遠有「現場」要收的狗主人——他會邊笑邊嘆氣
variations: 精簡版：我家狗不是在搗蛋，牠是在用牠的方式提醒我，這個家還需要牠。
延伸版：罪證確鑿、現場一片狼藉，牠卻搖著尾巴一臉驕傲。我只好承認：好啦，佈置得很有創意。

en.content:
My dog has one magical talent:

the moment I'm in a good mood, the house is spotless, and the day's going great,
that is the exact moment
it turns an entire roll of toilet paper into a snowstorm.

Then it stands in the middle of the scene,
tail wagging at full power,
eyes saying: "Look! I redecorated for you!"

And you can't even get mad,
because it genuinely believes it did something wonderful.
en.editorNote: Send to the dog owner whose home always has a 'scene' to clean up — they'll laugh and sigh
variations: Short version: my dog isn't being bad. It's reminding me, in its own way, that this house still needs it.
Bonus: caught red-pawed, wreckage everywhere, and it's still wagging with pride. Fine. Very creative interior design.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗教會人活在當下的哲學，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗狗不會記得昨天你兇了牠，
也不會擔心明天會不會被丟下。

牠只活在「現在」——
現在你回來了，
現在有陽光，
現在你願意陪牠走這一段路。

我們花了好多錢上課、看書，
想學會「活在當下」。

結果家裡那隻，
早就用一輩子在示範給我們看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給總是被昨天的後悔、明天的焦慮綁住的朋友
variations: 精簡版：狗狗不會內耗，因為牠把所有的力氣都拿來愛現在的你。
延伸版：牠不問你過去做錯什麼，也不問你未來能給牠什麼。牠只在乎，這一刻你在不在。

en.content:
