and it will still look up at you, eyes shining,
with a face that says:

"Wow, you're so right,
how did I never think of that!"

In this whole life,
it might be the only one who believes in you
with zero conditions attached.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who got second-guessed all day and came home to a dog that's fully on their side — they'll grin
variations: Bonus: you rant about how impossible your boss is, it understands not a single word, but it nods twice and makes you feel completely justified.
Short version: the whole world can doubt you. Your dog never will.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬厭世表情走紅的網路梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
看你們全家換衣服、拿鑰匙、找包包，
柴犬坐在門口，
露出一個看破紅塵的表情。

那個眼神不是傷心，
是一種「又要被留在家了」的認命，
像在思考接下來這幾個小時的狗生，
到底要用什麼姿勢躺完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養柴犬、或任何一出門就被狗用厭世眼神目送的主人——太有畫面了
variations: 進階版：牠不吵不鬧，只是深深看你一眼，那一眼的資訊量比一篇小論文還多。
短版：你拿鑰匙的那一秒，柴犬的臉已經先幫你把「掰掰」寫好了。

en.content:
As your whole family gets dressed, grabs the keys, hunts for a bag,
the Shiba sits by the door
wearing the face of someone who has seen through all worldly things.

That look isn't sadness.
It's the quiet resignation of "here we go, left home again,"
like it's already deciding
which pose to spend the next few hours lying in.
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner — or anyone sent off by a world-weary dog stare every time they leave — the image is instant
variations: Bonus: no barking, no fuss, just one deep look that carries more information than an entire essay.
Short version: the second you pick up the keys, the Shiba's face has already written your goodbye for you.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 狗不會問你今天過得好不好，牠只是靜靜坐到你腳邊，用陪伴代替所有的問候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合分享寵物陪伴的溫暖時刻，或安慰情緒低落的朋友。
variations: 牠不需要理由就靠了過來，這大概就是最單純的愛。
有些安慰不用開口，一隻狗趴在你身邊就夠了。

en.content: A dog never asks how your day went. It just quietly settles at your feet and lets its company do the talking.
en.editorNote: Great for posts about the comfort of pets or cheering up a friend.
variations: He didn't need a reason to lean in close. Maybe that's the purest kind of love.
Some comfort needs no words. A dog curled up beside you says it all.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 想知道無條件的快樂長什麼樣子，看看你家的狗聽到「散步」兩個字的反應就懂了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配狗狗興奮衝向門口的照片或影片。
variations: 世界上最容易被討好的，大概就是聽到出門聲音的狗。
一句『走囉』就能讓牠原地起飛，這種快樂真的很療癒。

en.content: Want to know what unconditional joy looks like? Just say the word walk and watch your dog completely lose it.
en.editorNote: Pairs well with a video of a dog bolting for the door.
variations: Nothing on earth is easier to please than a dog who heard the leash jingle.
One 'let's go' and he's airborne. That kind of happiness is contagious.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 牠陪你的時間只是牠一生的全部，而你要記得，那份全心全意，值得被好好珍藏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合追憶離世毛孩，或提醒飼主珍惜當下。
variations: 對狗來說，你就是牠的整個世界，別讓牠等太久。
牠給的是一輩子，我們能回報的，是好好陪牠走完每一天。

en.content: You're only part of your dog's life, but to them you're the whole of it. That kind of devotion deserves to be treasured.
en.editorNote: For remembering a pet who passed, or reminding owners to cherish the now.
variations: To a dog, you are the entire world. Don't keep them waiting too long.
They give us a lifetime. The least we can do is be there for every day of it.

items.sourceNote: 外星人看人類遛狗撿便便的梗，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
如果外星人來地球考察，看到一個人跟在狗後面、彎腰把牠的大便裝進袋子裡……

牠們大概會在報告上寫：這顆星球，狗是老大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 遛狗撿便便的日常最有共鳴，養狗群組貼這則保證一片哈哈
variations: 延伸版：而且那個人還會一手拿袋子、一手滑手機，全程沒有一句怨言——服從性滿分。
所以到底誰才是被馴養的那一方？我拒絕回答這個問題。

en.content:
If aliens landed on Earth and watched a human trailing behind a dog, bending down to bag up its poop…

they'd probably write in their report: on this planet, the dogs are clearly in charge.
en.editorNote: The poop-bag walk is peak relatable — drop this in any dog owner group chat
variations: Bonus: and the human does it one-handed while scrolling their phone, not a single complaint. Full marks for obedience.
So remind me again — which one of us actually got domesticated?

items.sourceNote: 我不是寵壞狗、是我被訓練得很好，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
別人說我把狗寵壞了。

才不是。

是牠把我訓練得很好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 自嘲型金句，適合當養狗貼文的短標題或圖片配字
variations: 牠一個眼神，我就自動起身去開罐頭——這叫默契，不叫寵壞。
家裡真正的主子從來不是我，我只是負責付帳單的那個。

en.content:
People say I've spoiled my dog.

I haven't.

My dog has trained me very well.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating one-liner — great as a caption or on a photo overlay
variations: One look from her and I'm already up cracking open a can. That's not spoiling, that's teamwork.
The real boss of this house was never me. I just handle the bills.

items.sourceNote: 狗不用說話就能把你修好，改寫自 Caroline George 語意

zh-tw.content:
你難過的時候，牠不會問發生什麼事，也不會給你建議。

牠只是把頭靠過來，重重地壓在你腿上。

然後你就發現，其實你需要的，一直只是這樣而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合心情低落時分享，或送給正經歷難關的養狗朋友
variations: 狗大概是這世界上，唯一不用開口就能把你修好的存在。
牠聽不懂你在說什麼，卻永遠知道你什麼時候不好。

en.content:
When you're falling apart, your dog doesn't ask what happened. It doesn't offer advice.

It just leans its head over and rests it, heavy, on your leg.

And somehow you realize that's all you ever needed.
en.editorNote: For a low day, or for a dog-owner friend going through something hard
variations: A dog might be the only thing on earth that can fix you without saying a single word.
It never understands what you're saying, but it always knows exactly when you're not okay.

items.sourceNote: 掉在地上的食物不用撿，因為有狗，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我有點同情家裡沒養狗的人。

聽說東西掉到地上，他們還得自己彎腰去撿。
zh-tw.editorNote: 掉食物秒清空的養狗日常，貼文或限動的短笑點
variations: 自從養狗以後，我家的地板乾淨到可以直接躺——牠比掃地機器人還勤勞。
五秒規則？在我家是零點五秒規則，而且執行者不是我。

en.content:
I feel a little sorry for people who don't have a dog.

Apparently when they drop food on the floor, they have to pick it up themselves.
en.editorNote: The instant floor-cleanup life — a quick laugh for a post or story
variations: Since getting a dog, my floor is clean enough to lie on. She works harder than any robot vacuum.
Five-second rule? In my house it's the half-second rule, and I'm not the one enforcing it.

items.sourceNote: 狗會算數，知道冰箱裡還剩幾片起司，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
別說狗不會算數。

我打開冰箱，牠就坐得直挺挺，
眼睛盯著我的手——牠清楚記得裡面還剩三片起司，
而且知道那三片，理論上有牠一份。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在餵食日常的貼文或限動，養狗人一看就笑
variations: 牠不懂微積分，但只要塑膠袋一有聲音，牠立刻算出這是不是零食等級的響動。
我以為牠在睡覺，其實牠在計時——距離我上次分牠食物，已經過了整整四十分鐘。

en.content:
Don't tell me dogs can't do math.

I open the fridge and she sits up perfectly straight,
eyes locked on my hands — she knows there are exactly three slices of cheese left,
and she's certain at least one of them is, by law, hers.
en.editorNote: Perfect caption for a feeding-time post or story; every dog owner will laugh
variations: She can't do calculus, but the second a plastic bag crinkles she instantly calculates whether it's snack-grade.
I thought she was asleep. She was actually timing me — it's been a full forty minutes since I last shared anything.

items.sourceNote: 抱歉我去不了，我家狗把頭靠在我腿上，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
「今晚要一起出來嗎？」

我本來想去的。

但就在我要回訊息的那一刻，
牠把頭輕輕放到我腿上，嘆了一口氣。

這局，我輸得心甘情願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 推掉聚會時傳給朋友的最佳藉口，養狗人都懂這種幸福的被綁架
variations: 我人生最常用的請假理由：我家狗現在的姿勢，我不忍心打擾。
別人被沙發黏住，我是被一隻三十公斤的毛球黏住，而且我完全不想掙脫。

en.content:
"You coming out tonight?"

I genuinely meant to.

But right as I was about to reply,
she rested her head on my lap and let out a little sigh.

So that's that. Gladly defeated.
en.editorNote: The ultimate text to send when bailing on plans — dog people know this happy hostage situation
variations: My most-used excuse in life: I can't move, the dog is in a pose I refuse to disturb.
Some people get stuck to the couch. I get stuck under thirty kilos of fur, and I have no interest in escaping.

items.sourceNote: 外星人看人類撿狗大便會以為誰是主人，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
如果外星人來到地球，
看到人類彎腰幫狗撿大便、
狗在旁邊悠閒地聞花——

他們大概會很認真地思考：
這兩種生物，到底是誰在養誰？
zh-tw.editorNote: 散步日常的哲學小笑點，配一張撿便的照片超有梗
variations: 我每天早上準時起床、風雨無阻出門、隨手備好牽繩和便袋——這種紀律，我只為一位主管做到，牠有四條腿。
牠一句話都沒說，我卻自動自發地服務牠的一天。這不是主人，這是老闆。

en.content:
If aliens landed on Earth
and saw humans bending down to pick up dog poop
while the dog calmly sniffed a flower nearby —

they'd probably think very hard about it
and ask: so which of these two is actually in charge?
en.editorNote: A philosophical little laugh for walk-time; pairs perfectly with a poop-bag photo
variations: I get up on time every morning, head out rain or shine, leash and bags ready — I only show this kind of discipline for one boss, and she has four legs.
She hasn't said a single word, yet I voluntarily run her entire schedule. That's not an owner. That's an employee.

items.sourceNote: 狗讓我們的生活變得完整，Roger Caras 名言改寫

zh-tw.content:
狗不是你生活的全部。

牠不會幫你賺錢、不會替你解決煩惱，
甚至常常把你的一天搞得亂七八糟。

但下班回家、開門的那一刻你就懂了——
少了牠，這個家會空掉一塊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放在深夜的暖心貼文，或送給剛失去毛孩的朋友
variations: 牠佔的空間不大，但牠一走，整間屋子都會顯得太安靜。
有人問我養狗值不值得。我沒有回答，只是想到牠等我回家的樣子，就笑了。

en.content:
A dog isn't your whole life.

She won't earn you money, won't solve your problems,
and honestly wrecks your schedule more often than not.

But the moment you come home and open the door, you understand —
without her, this place would have an empty space in it.
en.editorNote: A warm late-night post, or something gentle to send a friend who just lost their dog
variations: She doesn't take up much room, but the day she's gone, the whole house feels too quiet.
Someone asked if having a dog is worth it. I didn't answer — I just pictured her waiting at the door, and smiled.

items.sourceNote: 狗教你無條件的愛，Robert Wagner 名言改寫

zh-tw.content:
狗這輩子沒讀過任何一本教你如何愛人的書。

但牠比誰都懂——
不管你今天成功還是失敗、心情好還是壞、
身上有沒有味道，

牠愛你的方式，永遠只有一種：全部。
zh-tw.editorNote: 低潮時給自己的一點安慰，也適合當療癒系語錄貼文
variations: 你可以對全世界隱藏你的糟糕，但你不用對牠隱藏——因為牠早就決定，無論如何都選你。
牠不在乎你是不是最好的版本，牠只在乎你是不是回來了。

en.content:
Your dog has never read a single book on how to love someone.

Yet she understands it better than anyone —
whether you won or lost today, whether you're in a good mood or a wreck,
whether you smell great or not,

she loves you in exactly one way: completely.
en.editorNote: A little comfort for yourself on a low day, and a lovely healing-style post
variations: You can hide your worst from the whole world. You don't have to hide it from her — she decided a long time ago to choose you no matter what.
She doesn't care whether you're the best version of yourself. She only cares whether you came home.

items.sourceNote: 養狗後床鋪主權轉移的日常梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
感情狀態：睡覺睡到縮在床角，只為了不吵到狗。

這張床明明是你買的、你付的房租，
但事實上，你只是租了角落那一小塊。

真正的屋主，四腳朝天，睡得比誰都香。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天被狗擠到床邊的朋友——他半夜醒來就是這個姿勢
variations: 升級版：你想翻身，先跟牠預約。牠不同意，你就繼續維持那個會落枕的角度到天亮。
你以為你在養狗，其實你是在服侍一位睡姿霸道的房東。

en.content:
Relationship status: curled up in the corner of the bed so I don't disturb the dog.

You bought this bed. You pay the rent.

But in practice, you're just renting that one small corner —
while the real owner sleeps belly-up, out cold, better than anyone.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets edged off their own mattress every single night
variations: Upgrade: want to roll over? You'll need to book an appointment. If the dog says no, you hold that neck-cramping angle till sunrise.
You thought you got a dog. Turns out you got a landlord with a very aggressive sleeping position.

items.sourceNote: 狗弄壞東西卻一臉無辜的經典場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
沙發被咬爛、鞋子少一隻、垃圾桶倒在地上。

你轉頭看牠。

牠回你一個純真到發光的眼神，
彷彿在說：這一定是別人幹的，我剛好路過。

家裡明明只有你們兩個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有「破壞王」的狗主人——那個無辜臉他每天都要看一次
variations: 進階版：你拿著證物走過去，牠不但不心虛，還開心地搖尾巴，覺得你要陪牠玩。
牠的邏輯很簡單：只要我不承認，這件事就沒有發生。

en.content:
Couch shredded. One shoe missing. Trash can knocked over.

You turn and look at your dog.

It gives you a gaze so pure it practically glows,
as if to say: must've been someone else, I just happened to be walking by.

There are only the two of you in this house.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone living with a resident 'destruction expert' — they see that innocent face daily
variations: Advanced: you walk over holding the evidence, and instead of looking guilty, it wags its tail — clearly it thinks you're here to play.
Its logic is simple: if I never admit it, it never happened.

items.sourceNote: 狗的社群追蹤數超越主人的現代梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗在 IG 的粉絲比我還多。

牠一張躺在陽光下的睡照，破千讚。
我認真寫的貼文，三個讚，其中一個是我媽。

我現在不是牠的主人，
我是牠的經紀人兼專屬攝影師。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個手機相簿九成都是狗的朋友——他早就默默接受這個身分了
variations: 升級版：出門玩，路人不看你，先蹲下來拍牠。你只負責在旁邊拉牽繩，順便當背景。
你終於懂了：這個家真正的網紅不是你，你只是後勤團隊。

en.content:
My dog has more followers than I do.

One nap photo in a sunbeam? Over a thousand likes.
My carefully written post? Three likes — one of them is my mom.

I'm not its owner anymore.
I'm its manager and personal photographer.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose camera roll is 90% dog — they've quietly accepted this role already
variations: Upgrade: out in public, strangers ignore you and crouch to photograph the dog. Your job is holding the leash and being the background.
You finally get it: the real influencer in this house isn't you. You're just the support crew.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「狗讓生活變得完整」的經典概念，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗不是你生活的全部。

你有工作、有煩惱、有一堆待辦清單，
牠不會幫你解決任何一件。

但奇怪的是——
有了牠之後，那些原本破破的、缺一角的日子，
就這樣，被牠一塊一塊補了起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當療癒系語錄貼文，或分享給剛養狗、開始覺得生活不一樣的朋友
variations: 牠佔的不是你全部的時間，牠佔的是你回家時，那顆終於鬆下來的心。
牠沒讓你的人生變簡單，牠只是讓每一個平凡的傍晚，變得值得回家。

en.content:
A dog isn't your whole life.

You've got work, worries, a to-do list a mile long,
and your dog won't fix a single one of them.

But here's the strange part —
after it showed up, all those cracked, missing-a-corner days
quietly got pieced back together, one at a time.
en.editorNote: Perfect as a healing-style post, or to share with someone who just got a dog and feels life shifting
variations: It doesn't take up all your time. It takes up that spot in your chest that finally unclenches the moment you get home.
It didn't make your life simpler. It just made every ordinary evening worth coming home to.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「狗愛你勝過愛自己」的經典語句，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
這世界上，大概只有狗，
愛你愛到勝過愛牠自己。

最後一口食物，牠會分你。
最舒服的位置，牠讓給你。
就算你只是心情不好、什麼都沒說，
牠也會默默走過來，把頭靠在你腿上。

沒有理由，沒有條件，只因為那是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 低潮時給自己看一眼，也很適合配上狗把頭靠在你腿上的那種照片
variations: 牠不是不懂得為自己著想，牠只是把你排在自己前面，而且從來沒後悔過。
你或許不是最好的人，但在牠的世界裡，你永遠排第一，沒有第二名。

en.content:
In this whole world, maybe only a dog
loves you more than it loves itself.

The last bite of food? It'll share it.
The comfiest spot? It gives it to you.
Even when you're just quietly having a bad day and say nothing,
it walks over and rests its head on your knee.

No reason, no conditions — only because it's you.
en.editorNote: One to look at on a low day; pairs perfectly with a photo of a dog resting its head on your knee
variations: It's not that it doesn't know how to look out for itself. It just puts you first, and has never once regretted it.
You might not be the best person out there, but in its world you're first place, with no runner-up.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「狗以為主人是偉大獵人」的幽默觀點，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是去超市買菜，提著一堆雞肉、豬肉、半頭牛回家。

但在狗狗眼裡，你剛從野外空手打了三隻獵物凱旋而歸。

牠在門口跳來跳去，眼神閃閃發亮，
彷彿在說：我的主人是全世界最強的獵人。

牠不知道你只是刷了卡，還買一送一。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次買菜回家、狗都超級興奮的朋友——他一定笑出來
variations: 牠永遠不會知道，你的獵場叫做「量販店」，你的武器是一張會嗶嗶叫的卡。
在狗的世界觀裡，你是頂級掠食者。這個誤會，就讓它美好地延續下去吧。

en.content:
You just went to the supermarket and came home with chicken, pork, half a cow.

But in your dog's eyes, you returned from the wild after taking down three beasts bare-handed.

It bounces at the door, eyes sparkling,
as if to say: my human is the greatest hunter in the world.

It has no idea you just swiped a card and it was buy-one-get-one-free.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose dog loses its mind every time they walk in with grocery bags
variations: It'll never know your hunting ground is called 'the wholesale store' and your weapon is a card that beeps.
In your dog's worldview, you are an apex predator. Let's just let that beautiful misunderstanding continue.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「stay 是建議不是命令」的幽默觀點，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你對狗說「坐下」，牠坐了——這是奇蹟。

你說「等一下」，牠聽成「參考看看」。
你說「不可以」，牠翻譯成「等你轉頭再說」。
你說「趴下」，牠只把下巴放地上，其餘部位保留最終解釋權。

原來這幾年，我養的不是狗，是一位很有主見的室友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個「明明教過一百次牠還是自己有想法」的狗主人
variations: 在牠的字典裡，指令分兩種：牠想配合的，跟牠當作耳邊風的。
你以為你在下命令，其實你是在提案，而牠每次都行使否決權。

en.content:
You tell your dog to sit, and it sits — a genuine miracle.

You say 'stay,' and it hears 'something to consider.'
You say 'no,' and it translates that to 'wait until you turn around.'
You say 'lie down,' and it puts its chin on the floor while reserving the right to interpret the rest.

Turns out these past few years I didn't raise a dog. I raised a roommate with very strong opinions.
en.editorNote: For the owner who's taught the same command a hundred times and the dog still has its own ideas
variations: In its dictionary there are two kinds of commands: the ones it feels like following, and the ones it treats as background noise.
You think you're giving orders. You're actually making proposals, and it vetoes every single one.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「外星人看誰在撿便便誰是主人」的幽默觀點，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果外星人第一次來地球，看到人牽著狗散步——

狗負責大搖大擺往前走，決定路線、決定停多久、決定聞哪根電線桿。
人負責跟在後面，還彎腰把狗的便便撿起來裝進袋子帶走。

外星人回去一定會報告：
那顆星球上，體型小、有毛、四隻腳的才是老大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 散步撿便便時很有感，傳給每個「以為自己是主人」的狗奴
variations: 牽繩另一端是誰在領路，其實我們心裡都有數。
帳面上你是飼主，實際上你是保鑣兼清潔員兼行程配合者。

en.content:
If aliens landed on Earth for the first time and saw a human walking a dog —

the dog struts ahead, picking the route, deciding how long to stop, choosing which pole to sniff.
The human trails behind, bends down, scoops up the dog's poop, and carries it away in a bag.

Those aliens would fly home and report:
on that planet, the small furry four-legged ones are clearly in charge.
en.editorNote: Hits hard mid-walk with a poop bag in hand; send to any dog owner who thinks they're the boss
variations: We all secretly know exactly who's leading whom on the other end of that leash.
On paper you're the owner. In practice you're the bodyguard, the janitor, and the schedule's most flexible member.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「狗讓我們的人生變得完整」的溫暖語句，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗不是你人生的全部，
但牠會讓你原本零零散散的人生，變得完整。

牠不會多做什麼，
就是在你回家時等在門口，
在你發呆時把頭靠過來，
在你以為沒人在乎的時候，用整條尾巴告訴你：有，我在。

有些空缺，剛好是一隻狗的形狀。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配上狗在門口等你的照片，或送給剛把毛孩接回家的朋友
variations: 牠補上的不是時間，而是那個你一直沒說出口的、「想有人在家等我」的心願。
在遇見牠之前，你以為你的生活已經很完整了；遇見之後，你才知道原來還缺這一塊。

en.content:
A dog isn't your whole life,
but it takes your scattered, in-between life and makes it whole.

It doesn't do much —
just waits at the door when you come home,
rests its head on you when you're staring into space,
and right when you think nobody cares, tells you with its entire tail: someone does. I'm here.

Some gaps in a life are exactly the shape of a dog.
en.editorNote: Pairs beautifully with a photo of a dog waiting at the door; a lovely note for someone who just brought a pup home
variations: What it fills isn't your time — it's that quiet, unspoken wish to have someone waiting when you get home.
Before you met it, you thought your life was already complete. After, you realized this was the piece that had been missing.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗盯著人吃東西的幽默觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
如果我家狗有正職，牠的職稱一定是「零食督導」。

你開冰箱——牠到。
你撕開餅乾——牠到。
你半夜偷吃一口，以為神不知鬼不覺——
黑暗中，兩顆反光的眼睛已經在監督你了。

牠不是在乞討，牠是在確認你有沒有照 SOP 分牠一口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次吃東西都被狗盯到有壓力的朋友，配一張狗狗直勾勾盯著鏡頭的照片最有戲
variations: 牠對你的作息瞭若指掌，唯一的漏洞就是：只要有食物出現，牠的雷達永遠比你快 0.5 秒。
你以為你養了一隻狗，其實你請了一位 24 小時無休、專門盯零食的稽核員。

en.content:
If my dog had a real job, the title on the business card would read: Snack Supervisor.

You open the fridge — it's there.
You tear open a bag of chips — it's there.
You sneak one bite at 2 a.m., certain nobody saw —
two glowing eyes are already auditing you from the dark.

It's not begging. It's checking whether you followed the standard operating procedure and saved it a piece.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't eat a single snack in peace; hits hardest with a photo of a dog staring dead into the camera
variations: It has memorized your entire daily routine, and the only glitch in the system is this: the moment food appears, its radar beats yours by half a second.
You thought you adopted a dog. You actually hired a 24/7 snack-compliance auditor who never takes a day off.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗愛管閒事、監視鄰居的幽默觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我家狗的兩大興趣：

一、趴在窗邊，審判每一個路過的鄰居。
二、舔地板上那個連我都看不見的東西。

宅配來按門鈴，牠叫得像社區出了大事；
真的小偷來，牠大概會先幫忙開門帶位。

這隻狗的判斷力，我到現在還是搞不懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養了一隻「叫聲很兇但其實超膽小」的狗的朋友，他會秒懂
variations: 牠對郵差有深仇大恨，對真正的陌生人卻熱情到讓人擔心——牠的警戒系統顯然裝反了。
在牠的世界裡，最危險的生物是掃地機器人，最無害的是任何願意摸牠肚子的人。

en.content:
My dog has exactly two hobbies:

One — lying by the window, passing judgment on every neighbor who walks by.
Two — licking a spot on the floor that even I can't see.

When a delivery guy rings the bell, it barks like the whole block is under attack.
If an actual burglar showed up, it would probably hold the door and show them around.

I have given up trying to understand this dog's judgment.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose dog sounds terrifying but is secretly a total coward — instant recognition
variations: It has a personal vendetta against the mail carrier, yet greets genuine strangers with a warmth that honestly worries me. Its threat-detection is clearly installed backwards.
In its world, the most dangerous creature alive is the robot vacuum, and the most harmless is anyone willing to rub its belly.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗無條件的愛與信任，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你這一天過得多糟，牠不知道。
你搞砸了什麼、被誰誤解、對著手機嘆了幾次氣，牠都不知道。

牠只知道一件事：
是你回來了。

然後牠用整個身體告訴你——
在這扇門後面，你不必是誰、不必表現得好，
光是「你回來了」這件事，就值得牠全世界最盛大的歡迎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近很累、覺得自己哪裡都做不好的朋友，提醒他有一種愛不需要他變得更好
variations: 人會因為你的成敗改變眼神，狗不會——牠愛的一直都只是「你」這個版本，不是「更好的你」。
牠給你的信任是滿的，沒有條件、沒有前提。這種愛得來不易，別辜負它。

en.content:
Your dog has no idea how bad your day was.
It doesn't know what you messed up, who misread you, or how many times you sighed at your phone.

It knows one thing, and one thing only:
you came home.

And it tells you with its whole body —
behind this door, you don't have to be anyone or perform for anybody.
The simple fact that you walked back in earns you the biggest welcome in the world.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's worn out and feels like they can't get anything right — a reminder that some love asks nothing of you
variations: People's eyes shift with your wins and losses. A dog's never do — it loves the you that showed up, not a better version of you.
The trust it hands you is total: no conditions, no fine print. That kind of love is rare. Don't ever betray it.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗一生短暫但全心陪伴，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠的一輩子，大概就是你的十幾年。

對你來說，那是升學、換工作、搬家、談了幾場戀愛的一段人生。
對牠來說，那是全部。

牠把整個「全部」都給了你——
沒有留一手、沒有想過以後、沒有為自己算計過一次。

所以請在牠還在的時候，多陪牠走幾步路。
因為你有很多年，而牠，只有你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 溫柔提醒型語錄，適合傳給家裡有狗、尤其是狗狗漸漸變老的朋友，配一張散步背影照最戳心
variations: 牠不會計較你今天忙不忙、累不累，牠只是把每一次你願意蹲下來抱牠的瞬間，當成一生的大事。
你會遇見很多人、去很多地方；而牠的世界，從頭到尾就只有一個你。這份重量，值得被好好接住。

en.content:
Its whole life is maybe a dozen of your years.

To you, that stretch holds school, a new job, a move or two, a few loves that came and went.
To it, that stretch is everything.

And it gives you the entire everything —
nothing held back, no thought of later, not one moment spent looking out for itself.

So while it's still here, walk a few more blocks with it.
Because you have many years, and it has only you.
en.editorNote: A gentle, tender one; send to a friend with a dog, especially an aging one — devastating paired with a photo of a walk from behind
variations: It never keeps score of how busy or tired you were today. It just treats every moment you kneel down to hold it as the event of a lifetime.
You'll meet many people and see many places. Its world, from beginning to end, is just one you. That weight deserves to be held gently.

items.sourceNote: 養狗才懂的日常荒謬，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
當初想著要養一隻寵物，
結果養進來的是一位主管。

幾點該吃飯、幾點該散步、你可不可以坐這張沙發，
通通牠說了算。

而你，只是那個負責付薪水、還常常被嫌不夠專業的員工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配一張狗狗一臉嚴肅盯著你的照片，養狗的人看了都會笑著點頭
variations: 你以為你是飼主，其實你是助理；行程表是牠排的，你只負責執行。
牠不會開會，但每一項決策——散步路線、零食份量、誰睡床中間——都由牠拍板。

en.content:
You thought you were getting a pet.

Turns out you hired a manager.

When you eat, when you walk, whether you're allowed on that couch —
all of it is their call now.

And you? You're just the employee who pays the bills and still gets side-eyed for underperforming.
en.editorNote: Pairs perfectly with a photo of your dog staring you down; every owner will laugh and nod
variations: You assumed you were the owner. You're the assistant. They set the schedule, you just execute it.
They never call a meeting, but every decision — the walk route, the treat portion, who sleeps in the middle of the bed — gets their final sign-off.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗無條件的愛，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
牠不在乎你今天賺多少、穿得體不體面、有沒有把人生過成別人眼中的樣子。

你一進門，牠看你的眼神，
跟你最風光那天一模一樣。

在這個什麼都要條件的世界裡，
只有牠，把你當成不用理由就值得的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 低潮的時候最療癒的一段，適合傳給覺得自己不夠好的朋友
variations: 全世界都在幫你打分數的時候，牠是唯一一個從頭到尾給你滿分的。
你在別人面前要努力證明自己，只有在牠面前，你什麼都不用是，就已經足夠。

en.content:
It doesn't care how much you made today, whether you dressed the part, or if your life looks the way people expect it to.

The way it looks at you the second you walk in
is exactly how it looked at you on your best day ever.

In a world that asks you to qualify for everything,
it's the one who decided you're worth it with no reason at all.
en.editorNote: The most comforting one for a low day — send to a friend who feels like they're not enough
variations: While the whole world is busy scoring you, it's the only one handing you a perfect ten from start to finish.
Everywhere else you have to prove yourself. In front of it, you don't have to be anything — you're already enough.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛與相機膠捲的日常，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
養狗以後，你的人生就變成這樣：

每個包包裡都藏著一支黏毛滾筒，
手機相簿有九成都是同一隻狗的照片，
跟朋友的約，永遠可能因為牠一個眼神就取消。

你嘴上喊著好累，
心裡卻沒有一天想把這些換掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 養狗人的真實日常，配一張滿是狗照的相簿截圖超有共鳴
variations: 衣服上永遠有牠的毛，行程永遠可以為牠讓路，這就是當毛孩爸媽的日常。
別人問你最近在忙什麼，你打開相簿——好，全都是牠，一張你自己的都沒有。

en.content:
Once you get a dog, your life quietly turns into this:

a lint roller stashed in every bag,
a camera roll that's ninety percent the same dog,
and plans that can fall apart the second it gives you that look.

You keep saying you're exhausted,
but there's not a single day you'd trade any of it away.
en.editorNote: The real daily life of a dog parent — hits hard with a screenshot of a camera roll full of dog photos
variations: There's always fur on your shirt and your schedule always makes room for it. That's the whole job of being a dog parent.
Someone asks what you've been up to, you open your photos — yep, all of it is the dog, not one shot of you.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗安靜的忠誠，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
牠不會說話，卻懂得在你最安靜的時候，靠過來。

那些沒被任何人注意到的低潮，
牠都在。

你這輩子聽過那麼多漂亮的承諾，
最後你發現，最重的那句「我不會走」，
是一隻不會說話的狗，用一整個晚上趴在你腳邊說完的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 安靜卻很有重量的一段，適合深夜情緒版位，配狗狗趴在腳邊的照片
variations: 牠沒有一句安慰的話，卻用整個身體告訴你：我在，你不是一個人。
別人用嘴巴陪你，牠用時間陪你——一整晚都不走，就是牠的說法。

en.content:
It can't say a word, yet it knows to come close exactly when you go quiet.

Every low moment no one else even noticed,
it was there.

You've heard so many beautiful promises in your life,
and in the end you realize the heaviest one — 'I'm not going anywhere' —
was said by a dog that couldn't speak, curled at your feet all night long.
en.editorNote: Quiet but heavy — good for a late-night emotional slot, paired with a photo of a dog resting at your feet
variations: It offers no words of comfort, yet its whole body tells you: I'm here, you're not alone.
Others keep you company with their mouths; it keeps you company with its time — staying all night is how it says it.

items.sourceNote: 外星人看人類撿大便誰是主人的梗，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
如果外星人第一次來地球，剛好看到你在遛狗——

牠在前面大搖大擺地走，你在後面彎腰把牠的大便撿起來，
然後恭恭敬敬地打包，放進自己的袋子裡帶走。

外星人一定會回報總部：
「這個星球的主人是四隻腳、會搖尾巴的那個。」

然後他們就對了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早晚要出門撿黃金的狗奴，他會笑著承認自己就是那個「僕人」
variations: 升級版：牠散步時停下來聞一根電線桿聞十分鐘，你就站在旁邊等。到底是誰在遛誰，這題我們心裡都有答案。
牠指定路線、指定速度、指定要在哪棵樹前停，你只負責付錢跟撿大便。這關係，說是主人也太謙虛了。

en.content:
If aliens landed on Earth for the first time and happened to catch you walking your dog —

it struts proudly out in front, while you bend down behind it, scoop up its poop,
wrap it neatly, and carry it away in your own bag.

The aliens would report straight back to command:
"The one in charge on this planet has four legs and a wagging tail."

And honestly, they'd be right.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone doing the twice-a-day poop patrol — they'll laugh and admit they're the staff, not the owner
variations: Upgrade: it stops to sniff one lamppost for ten minutes and you just stand there waiting. Who's walking whom? We all know the answer.
It picks the route, the pace, and which tree to stop at; your job is to pay the bills and carry the bag. Calling yourself the 'owner' is generous.

items.sourceNote: Dave Barry 狗狗當最佳聽眾的名言，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你可以對著狗說出全世界最蠢的一句話，

牠還是會抬頭看你，眼神發光，好像在說：
「哇，你講得也太有道理了，我怎麼從來沒想過！」

這就是為什麼那麼多人下班回家，
第一個想講話的對象不是人，是牠。

因為牠從不打斷你、不糾正你，也從不覺得你很煩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配一張狗狗歪頭專注聽你講話的照片，療癒又好笑
variations: 你抱怨主管、抱怨房租、抱怨天氣，牠全部認真聽完，還多送你一個舔手手。世界上哪找得到這種聽眾。
牠不會回你「所以你想怎樣」，牠只會靠過來趴在你腿上，告訴你：講完了嗎？講完我們就都沒事了。

en.content:
You can say the single dumbest thing in the world to your dog,

and it will still look up at you, eyes shining, as if to say:
"Wow, that is such a good point — how did I never think of that?"

That's exactly why so many people, after a long day,
want to talk to it before they talk to any human.

Because it never interrupts you, never corrects you, and never once finds you annoying.
en.editorNote: Pair with a photo of a dog tilting its head, listening intently — wholesome and funny
variations: You vent about your boss, your rent, the weather — it hears you out completely and throws in a free lick on the hand. Where else do you find a listener like that?
It never hits you with 'so what do you want to do about it.' It just climbs onto your lap as if to say: done venting? Good, now we're both fine.

items.sourceNote: 「當一個像我的狗以為的那麼好的人」名言，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子最大的目標，

是努力活成——我家的狗一直以為我是的那種人。

在牠眼裡，我勇敢、可靠、永遠會回家，
是全世界最值得等的那個人。

我知道我沒那麼好，
但只要牠還這樣看我，我就想再努力一點，別讓牠看走眼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 溫暖又帶點自省，適合深夜語錄版位，也很適合送給正在低潮的養狗朋友
variations: 牠把我想得太好了，好到我有點慚愧——但也因為這樣，我每天都想成為那個牠深信不疑的我。
別人看到我的缺點，只有牠，永遠只看到我最好的那一面。為了不辜負這份信任，我願意變好。

en.content:
The biggest goal of my life

is to become the person my dog already thinks I am.

In its eyes I'm brave, dependable, always coming home —
the one soul in the whole world most worth waiting for.

I know I'm not really that good,
but as long as it keeps looking at me that way, I want to try a little harder and prove it right.
en.editorNote: Warm with a touch of self-reflection — great for a late-night slot, or to send a dog-owning friend who's having a rough time
variations: It thinks far too highly of me — so highly it's almost embarrassing — but that's exactly why I want to become the me it believes in every single day.
Everyone else sees my flaws; only it sees my best side, always. To live up to that trust, I'm willing to become better.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗教會我們郵差不可信任的梗，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，我學到一個人生大道理：

這世界上最危險的人，是郵差。

每次門鈴一響，牠就衝到門口狂吠，
用生命警告我：外面有壞人，快躲起來。

然後我打開門，是我上禮拜自己網購的貓砂。

牠回頭看我，一臉「不客氣，我又救了你一次」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有看門狗、每天對外送和郵差開戰的朋友，超有共鳴
variations: 在牠的世界觀裡，宅配、外送、郵差、鄰居，全部都是潛在威脅。牠不是兇，牠只是保全上班太認真。
包裹送到門口的那一秒，牠已經替你擋下一場想像中的入侵。這份薪水我們用零食支付，牠也甘之如飴。

en.content:
Since getting a dog, I've learned one great truth about life:

the most dangerous person in the world is the mail carrier.

Every time the doorbell rings, it charges to the door barking its head off,
warning me with its whole life: there's a villain outside, hide now.

Then I open the door — it's the cat litter I ordered online last week.

It looks back at me with a face that says, "You're welcome. Saved you again."
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog declares war on every delivery and mail carrier — instantly relatable
variations: In its worldview, couriers, delivery drivers, mail carriers, and neighbors are all potential threats. It's not aggressive — it just takes the security job very seriously.
The second a package hits the doormat, it has already blocked an imaginary invasion on your behalf. We pay this salary in treats, and it's more than happy with the deal.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬個性獨立、喜歡保持距離的特性，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
別人家的狗黏得像口香糖，我家柴犬走的是「保持社交距離」路線。

你熱情叫牠，牠瞄你一眼，繼續趴在牠的地盤。
你想抱牠，牠優雅地起身走到房間另一邊。

但等到你難過、生病、一個人躲在角落，
牠會默默走過來，在你旁邊坐下——不貼太近，但也絕不走遠。

柴犬的愛，是有距離的，卻從來不缺席。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個養柴犬、常被牠嫌棄卻又在關鍵時刻被暖到的柴奴
variations: 牠平常對你愛理不理，一副厭世臉，但你一躺下，牠就悄悄在你腳邊卡位。柴犬式深情，就是嘴硬身體誠實。
牠不會撲上來討抱，牠只會在你看不到的角度默默守著你。距離是牠的個性，陪伴才是牠的本質。

en.content:
Other people's dogs cling like chewing gum. My Shiba prefers to keep a respectful social distance.

You call it warmly — it glances at you and stays flopped in its own spot.
You try to hug it — it elegantly gets up and moves to the other side of the room.

But the moment you're sad, sick, or hiding alone in a corner,
it quietly walks over and sits down beside you — not too close, but never far.

A Shiba's love keeps its distance, yet it never once fails to show up.
en.editorNote: Send to every Shiba owner who gets snubbed daily but melts the second their aloof dog shows up when it matters
variations: It ignores you all day with that world-weary face, but the moment you lie down, it silently claims the spot by your feet. Shiba devotion: cold mouth, honest body.
It won't leap up begging to be held; it just guards you quietly from an angle you can't see. Distance is its personality — showing up is its nature.

items.sourceNote: 「狗狗數學」——零食掰一半等於零顆，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，我才發現牠是數學天才。

我每次一開冰箱，牠就知道裡面還剩幾片起司。
我把一塊零食掰成兩半，在牠眼裡不是「兩顆」，是「零顆」——因為那樣不算數。
我給一顆，牠盯著我，意思是：起跳三顆，不然這局不算。

我懷疑牠上輩子是收帳的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個被狗用眼神逼著加碼零食的飼主，超中
variations: 牠分不清一加一，但牠精準記得你答應過牠幾次「最後一片」。這種選擇性數學，全世界的狗都會。
掉在地上的餅乾屑牠算得清清楚楚，你少給的那半顆牠也記在小本本上，改天用撒嬌跟你討回來。

en.content:
Since getting a dog, I've discovered it's a math genius.

Every time I open the fridge, it knows exactly how many slices of cheese are left.
Break one treat in half and, in its eyes, that isn't 'two' — it's zero, because halves don't count.
Give it one and it stares at me like: the opening bid is three, otherwise this round is void.

I'm starting to think it worked in debt collection in a past life.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who gets stared into raising the treat count — painfully accurate
variations: It can't do one plus one, but it remembers exactly how many times you promised it 'the last piece.' Every dog on earth aces this selective math.
It counts every crumb that hits the floor, and it keeps a little ledger of the half-treat you shortchanged it — to be collected later, with interest, in the form of puppy eyes.

items.sourceNote: 小型犬佔滿大床、飼主只租到一角，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我買了一張加大雙人床，想說睡起來一定超舒服。

結果現在的狀況是這樣：
一隻六公斤的狗，橫躺在正中央，佔了整張床的九成。
我縮在剩下的邊邊，一整晚維持同一個姿勢，不敢翻身，怕吵到牠。

這張床的房貸是我在繳，
但真正的屋主，是牠。我只是租了一個角落。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個明明買大床、卻睡成一條線的狗奴，一秒共鳴
variations: 牠睡得四腳朝天、打呼連連，我像壁虎一樣貼在床緣。這不是同床，這是我在牠的地盤借宿一晚。
科學無法解釋：為什麼一隻小型犬，可以把加大床睡成單人床。牠的睡姿有一種擴張領土的天賦。

en.content:
I bought a king-sized bed, figuring I'd finally sleep in total comfort.

Here's how it actually goes:
a six-kilogram dog lies sideways across the exact center, taking up ninety percent of it.
I curl up on the leftover sliver of edge, holding one position all night, afraid to roll over in case I disturb it.

I'm the one paying the mortgage on this bed,
but the real owner is the dog. I just rent a corner.
en.editorNote: Send to every dog owner who bought a huge bed and still sleeps in a single narrow line
variations: It sleeps belly-up, snoring away, while I cling to the mattress edge like a gecko. This isn't sharing a bed — I'm a guest crashing on its territory for the night.
Science can't explain it: how does one small dog turn a king-sized bed into a twin? Its sleeping posture has a real talent for annexing land.

items.sourceNote: 起身喝水回來、暖位被狗佔走，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
半夜起來喝口水，前後不到一分鐘。

回到床上——我剛剛睡熱的那個位置，已經被牠佔走了。
牠蜷在那塊還有餘溫的地方，睡得又香又理直氣壯。

我看著牠，本來想把牠搬開，
最後還是默默躺到旁邊冷冷的那邊。

算了，牠睡得香，我這一夜也就值得了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個半夜讓位給狗、自己去睡冷位置的傻氣飼主
variations: 牠不是故意的，牠只是天生會找全屋最溫暖的那一塊——通常那塊就是你剛離開的位置。
我常想，到底是我養牠，還是牠管我。但每次看牠睡得那麼安心，這個問題就不重要了。

en.content:
I got up in the middle of the night for a sip of water — gone less than a minute.

Came back to bed, and the warm spot I'd just been sleeping in? Already taken.
It's curled up right on the patch that still holds my body heat, sleeping soundly and completely unapologetic.

I look at it, fully intending to move it over,
and then quietly lie down on the cold empty side instead.

Never mind. It's sleeping peacefully, so the night was worth it anyway.
en.editorNote: Send to every softhearted owner who gives up the warm spot and takes the cold side of the bed
variations: It doesn't do it on purpose — it just has a gift for finding the warmest patch in the whole house, which usually happens to be wherever you just left.
I often wonder whether I own the dog or the dog manages me. But every time I see it sleeping that soundly, the question stops mattering.

items.sourceNote: 「外星人看人類撿狗大便，會以為誰才是主人」經典幽默，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
如果有外星人從天上觀察地球，
看到一個人牽著一隻狗散步，
狗在前面大搖大擺，人在後面彎腰、用手撿起狗剛剛拉的便便、還小心翼翼打包帶走——

請問，外星人會覺得這兩個之中，到底誰才是主人？

我想了很久，得出一個結論：
這問題不用問，答案很明顯，而且不是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個每天彎腰撿便、心裡其實很甘願的狗奴，笑中帶淚
variations: 我出門是牽狗散步，但整趟路是牠決定往哪走、走多久、在哪停下來研究一根電線桿。我只負責撿便和付錢。
從外星人的角度看，人類這個物種很奇怪：花錢養一個生物，還幫牠撿大便，臉上卻是心甘情願的笑容。

en.content:
If aliens were watching Earth from above,
and they saw a human walking a dog —
the dog strutting proudly in front, the human bent over behind it, picking up what the dog just did with a little bag and carefully carrying it away —

which of the two would the aliens assume is in charge?

I thought about it for a long time and reached a conclusion:
no need to ask. The answer is obvious, and it isn't me.
en.editorNote: Send to every owner who bends down to scoop poop daily and somehow doesn't mind at all
variations: I go out to walk the dog, but the dog decides where we go, how long we go, and which lamppost is worth ten minutes of investigation. I just handle the bags and the bills.
From an alien's point of view, humans are a strange species: they pay to keep a creature, pick up after it, and do it all with a perfectly willing smile.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗每天叫你早起、卻是最想見的鬧鐘，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠是我每天早起的原因。

真的很早——天還沒亮，鬧鐘還沒響，
牠就用濕濕的鼻子頂我的手，用尾巴拍打床沿，
提醒我：新的一天開始了，該起來陪我了。

以前我很討厭早起。
現在我發現，被一個那麼期待見到你的生命叫醒，
其實是一種很奢侈的幸福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個被狗當鬧鐘、嘴上抱怨心裡卻很暖的飼主
variations: 牠不會看時鐘，牠只知道天亮了、你還在、世界很美好，這三件事值得馬上叫醒你一起慶祝。
全世界的鬧鈴都讓人煩躁，只有牠這個鬧鐘，讓你睜開眼的第一秒就想微笑。

en.content:
It's the reason I wake up early every day.

And I mean early — before the sky lightens, before the alarm goes off,
it nudges my hand with its damp nose and thumps its tail against the side of the bed,
reminding me: a new day has started, time to get up and be with me.

I used to hate mornings.
Now I've realized that being woken up by a living thing that's this excited to see you
is actually an incredibly rare kind of happiness.
en.editorNote: Send to every owner whose dog is their alarm clock — the one wake-up call nobody truly resents
variations: It can't read a clock; it just knows the sun is up, you're still here, and the world is good — three things worth waking you up to celebrate right away.
Every alarm in the world is annoying, except this one — the only wake-up call that makes you smile the instant you open your eyes.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗自封廚房零食督導員，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗有一個沒人給牠的職位：廚房零食督導。

只要我一走進廚房，牠就自動就位，端正坐好，眼神鎖定我的每一個動作。

切菜——牠盯著。
開冰箱——牠站起來。
包裝袋發出「窸窣」一聲——牠瞳孔地震，彷彿聽見了命運的召喚。

牠什麼忙都幫不上，卻把「監督」這件事做到極致認真。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種一進廚房就有狗自動來報到的飼主，畫面感直接拉滿
variations: 牠的履歷上寫著：專長是站在你腳邊，用最誠懇的眼神提醒你，這一口其實可以分牠一半。
全世界最盡責的員工，就住在我家，薪水是一小塊起司，卻從不遲到早退。

en.content:
My dog holds a position nobody ever offered him: Snack Supervisor of the Kitchen.

The second I walk in, he clocks in — sits up straight, locks his eyes onto my every move.

Chopping vegetables — he's watching.
Opening the fridge — he's on his feet.
A bag goes 'crinkle' — his pupils quake, as if he's heard the call of destiny.

He can't help with a single thing, yet he takes the job of 'supervising' more seriously than anyone I've ever met.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose dog materializes the instant they enter the kitchen — instant mental image
variations: His resume says: skilled at standing by your feet and reminding you, with the sincerest eyes, that this bite could absolutely be split in half.
The most dedicated employee in the world lives in my house. Salary: one small cube of cheese. And he is never, ever late.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗把外送員當成專程來看牠的訪客，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
在我家狗的世界觀裡，門鈴響只有一個意思：

有人專程來看牠了。

外送員、快遞、水電工、隔壁鄰居——牠一律解讀成「粉絲見面會」。

牠衝到門口狂搖尾巴，準備好接受愛戴，
完全不知道對方只是來放個包裹、簽個名、三秒就走。

那種被瞬間冷落的表情，我每天都要安慰一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個門鈴一響、狗就以為全世界都來朝聖的飼主
variations: 對牠來說沒有陌生人，只有還沒開始崇拜牠的朋友。
牠始終相信，門外那個人不是來送東西的，是來確認牠今天有沒有變更可愛的。

en.content:
In my dog's worldview, the doorbell means exactly one thing:

someone has arrived specifically to see him.

Delivery driver, courier, plumber, the neighbor next door — he reads every single one as a personal meet-and-greet.

He sprints to the door, tail going wild, bracing himself to receive their adoration,
completely unaware that they're just dropping off a box, signing a slip, and leaving in three seconds.

That look of sudden heartbreak? I have to talk him down from it every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose dog treats every doorbell as the arrival of a devoted fan
variations: To him there are no strangers, only friends who haven't started worshipping him yet.
He remains convinced the person at the door isn't here to deliver anything, but to confirm whether he got even cuter today.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗把「等一下」當成參考建議而非命令，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
訓練師說，狗學會「等」就代表牠很聽話。

我家狗確實學會了「等」。

只是在牠的字典裡，「等一下」不是命令，是一個溫柔的參考建議——
聽是有聽到，做不做是另一回事。

我喊「等」，牠會停頓 0.5 秒，眼神跟我確認：
「你是認真的嗎？還是我們都心知肚明這只是形式？」

然後照樣去做牠本來想做的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個嘴上很兇、其實拿自家狗完全沒轍的飼主
variations: 牠不是不服從，牠只是把每一個指令都當成可以商量的開場白。
在牠眼中，「坐下」「等一下」「不可以」都是同一種語氣的建議，收不收全看牠今天心情。

en.content:
The trainer says a dog who's learned to 'stay' is an obedient dog.

My dog has, in fact, learned 'stay.'

It's just that in his dictionary, 'stay' isn't a command — it's a gentle suggestion for your consideration.
Heard, noted, and then filed under 'optional.'

When I say 'stay,' he pauses for half a second and checks my eyes:
'Are you serious right now? Or do we both know this is just a formality?'

And then he goes off and does exactly what he was already planning to do.
en.editorNote: Send to every owner who talks tough but is completely powerless against their own dog
variations: He isn't disobedient. He just treats every command as an opening line in a negotiation.
In his eyes, 'sit,' 'stay,' and 'no' are all the same kind of friendly suggestion — accepted or declined depending entirely on his mood.

items.sourceNote: 希望自己能有狗眼中一半的美好，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子最大的願望，
是有一天能真的成為，我家狗眼裡看到的那個人。

在牠眼中，我準時、我可靠、我回家就是全世界最好的事。
我隨手的一個摸頭，是牠一整天在等的高光時刻。

其實我知道自己沒那麼好——
我會累、會煩躁、會對著手機發呆到忘記時間。

但牠始終用那種眼神看我，好像我值得。
所以我也想努力一點，別辜負一雙那麼信任我的眼睛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得自己不夠好、卻被一隻狗無條件相信著的人
variations: 狗是這世界上唯一一個，把你高估到你都想活成牠以為的樣子的生命。
牠不知道我有多平凡，牠只知道我是牠的全部——光是這一點，就讓我想成為更好的人。

en.content:
The biggest wish of my whole life
is to one day actually become the person my dog already sees.

In his eyes I'm punctual, I'm dependable, and me coming home is the single best thing that will ever happen.
A distracted pat on the head is the highlight he's been waiting for all day.

The truth is, I know I'm not that great —
I get tired, I get short-tempered, I zone out at my phone until I lose track of time.

But he keeps looking at me like that, like I'm worthy of it.
So I want to try a little harder, and not let down a pair of eyes that trusts me this much.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who never feels good enough, yet is believed in unconditionally by one dog
variations: A dog is the only creature on earth that overestimates you so completely you actually want to grow into the version he already thinks you are.
He has no idea how ordinary I am; he only knows I'm his entire world — and honestly, that alone makes me want to be a better person.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗默默佔領整張沙發與床，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我一直以為這是我家。

直到養了狗我才發現，我只是租客，而且租約條件很差。

沙發最舒服的那個角落——牠的。
床中央黃金位置——牠的。
我剛坐熱的位子，我只是起來喝個水——回來就是牠的了。

我睡床邊十公分，蜷成一團，不敢亂動，
只因為不忍心吵醒一隻睡得那麼香、那麼理所當然的狗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個在自己床上被狗擠到邊緣、卻甘之如飴的飼主
variations: 牠不是佔領沙發，牠只是把整個家重新登記到自己名下，順便讓我保留使用權。
半夜醒來，發現自己掛在床邊，牠攤在正中央，四腳朝天——這一刻我才懂，誰才是這個家真正的主人。

en.content:
I always assumed this was my house.

Then I got a dog and realized I'm just a tenant — and the lease terms are terrible.

The comfiest corner of the couch — his.
The prime real estate in the middle of the bed — his.
The seat I just warmed up? I get up for a sip of water, and by the time I'm back, it's his too.

I sleep on ten centimeters of mattress edge, curled into a ball, afraid to move,
simply because I can't bring myself to wake a dog who's sleeping this soundly and this entitled.
en.editorNote: Send to every owner who's been squeezed to the edge of their own bed and somehow loves it
variations: He isn't taking over the couch — he's quietly re-registering the entire house under his name and generously letting me keep visiting rights.
You wake up at 3am dangling off the mattress while he's sprawled dead center, all four legs in the air — and that's the moment you finally understand who really owns this place.

items.sourceNote: 外星人看人類撿狗大便，誰才是主人的哏，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
假設有一天外星人來地球，
看到一個生物在前面優雅地散步，後面跟著一個生物，
彎腰、用袋子把前面那位的大便撿起來，還小心打包帶走。

外星人一定會在報告上寫：
「該星球的統治者體型較小，四條腿，由一種兩腳僕役貼身服侍。」

牠們沒有寫錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天彎腰撿便、還覺得心甘情願的鏟屎官——他會笑著承認自己就是那個僕役
variations: 我們以為自己在遛狗，其實是狗在帶我們出門放風。牽繩兩端，誰才是被牽著的那個，其實不太好說。
在狗的世界觀裡，人類存在的意義只有三個：開罐頭、開門、還有把地上那坨東西處理乾淨。

en.content:
Suppose aliens landed on Earth one day
and saw one creature strolling elegantly out front, with a second creature trailing behind,
bending down, scooping up the first one's poop into a bag, and carefully carrying it away.

They would absolutely write in their report:
"The rulers of this planet are small, four-legged, and personally attended by a species of two-legged servant."

They would not be wrong.
en.editorNote: Send to every owner who bends down for the bag daily and somehow feels grateful about it — they'll laugh and admit they're the servant
variations: We think we're walking the dog. Really, the dog is taking us out for some fresh air. On a leash with two ends, it's genuinely unclear who's being led.
In a dog's worldview, humans exist for exactly three reasons: open the can, open the door, and make that thing on the ground disappear.

items.sourceNote: 狗聽不懂坐下卻聽得懂零食的哏，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗的聽力很有選擇性。

「坐下」——牠望向遠方，彷彿在思考人生。
「過來」——牠突然對地板的某一點產生濃厚興趣。
「不可以」——牠正式進入失聰狀態。

但只要我在廚房，用氣音、隔著兩道門、輕聲說出「零食」兩個字，
牠會在零點三秒內出現在我腳邊，眼神清澈，彷彿從沒有聽不懂過任何一句話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個訓練狗狗屢屢失敗、卻對這種選擇性失聰又氣又好笑的飼主
variations: 狗不是笨，牠只是把有限的理解力，全部投資在跟吃有關的詞彙上。
牠聽不懂「安靜」「等一下」「放下」，但牠聽得懂開零食袋的聲音，還聽得懂我打開冰箱時那零點一秒的猶豫。

en.content:
My dog's hearing is remarkably selective.

"Sit" — he gazes into the distance, as if contemplating the meaning of life.
"Come" — he develops a sudden, profound interest in one specific spot on the floor.
"No" — he officially goes deaf.

But the moment I'm in the kitchen and whisper the word "snack" under my breath, through two closed doors,
he materializes at my feet in 0.3 seconds, eyes clear and bright, as if he has never once failed to understand a single word.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose training attempts keep failing and who finds this selective deafness equal parts infuriating and hilarious
variations: A dog isn't dumb. He just invests his entire, limited comprehension budget into food-related vocabulary.
He can't understand 'quiet,' 'wait,' or 'drop it,' but he understands the sound of a treat bag — and the half-second of hesitation when I open the fridge.

items.sourceNote: 家裡總有一個開門會開心的存在，改寫自 Nora Ephron 概念，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
人到了某個階段就會懂：
家裡有一隻狗，最珍貴的不是牠會什麼把戲，
而是不管你今天過得多爛，回到家，總有一個生命是真心為你開門而開心的。

你可能被主管罵、被朋友已讀、被整個世界冷落，
但你一轉鑰匙，牠就已經在門後蹦蹦跳跳，尾巴搖到快要離心飛出去。

在牠眼裡，你回來這件事，永遠是今天最好的消息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在外面撐了一整天、只想回家被無條件歡迎的人
variations: 全世界都可以對你冷淡，但只要家裡有狗，就永遠有一個為你開門會失控的存在。
長大後才明白，被一個生命毫無保留地期待著回家，本身就是一種很奢侈的幸福。

en.content:
At a certain point in life you understand:
the most precious thing about having a dog isn't the tricks it can do,
it's that no matter how badly your day went, when you come home there's always one living being genuinely thrilled that you walked through the door.

You might get chewed out by your boss, left on read by a friend, brushed off by the whole world,
but the second you turn the key, he's already bouncing behind the door, tail wagging so hard it might spin him off the ground.

In his eyes, you coming home is, without fail, the best news of the entire day.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who held it together all day and just wants to come home to an unconditional welcome
variations: The whole world can be cold to you, but as long as there's a dog at home, there's always one creature who loses its mind with joy just because you opened the door.
You only realize as an adult that being awaited, wholeheartedly and without reservation, is itself a wildly extravagant kind of happiness.

items.sourceNote: 狗填補了你不知道存在的空缺，改寫自 Thom Jones 概念，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗有一種本事，
總是能找到那個最需要牠的人，
然後默默填補一塊，連你自己都不知道空著的地方。

你以為自己過得好好的，一個人也沒什麼不行。
直到某個深夜，牠把頭靠在你腿上，重重嘆了一口氣，
你才突然發現，原來這麼久以來，你一直都很想要有一個誰，就這樣安靜地待在身邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個嘴上說一個人也好、卻在養狗之後才知道自己其實很需要陪伴的人
variations: 狗不是來取代誰的，牠是來補上那個你以為早就習慣的缺口。
你沒說出口的孤單，牠全都聽見了——所以牠選擇賴在你身邊，一步都不肯走遠。

en.content:
Dogs have this gift:
they always seem to find the one person who needs them most,
and quietly fill in a space you didn't even know was empty.

You thought you were doing fine, that being on your own was no big deal.
Then one late night, he rests his head on your leg and lets out a heavy sigh,
and you suddenly realize that for the longest time, you'd been aching for someone to just be there, quietly, at your side.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who insists they're fine alone but only understood how much they needed company after getting a dog
variations: A dog doesn't come to replace anyone. He comes to fill the gap you were sure you'd already gotten used to.
The loneliness you never said out loud, he heard all of it — which is exactly why he refuses to move even one step away from your side.

items.sourceNote: 狗比你更愛你自己，改寫自 Josh Billings 概念，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
這世界上，大概只有狗，
愛你的程度，比你愛自己還要多。

你會嫌自己不夠好、不夠瘦、不夠成功，
會對著鏡子挑剔，會在深夜懷疑自己是不是不值得被愛。

但牠從來沒有一秒懷疑過。
在牠心裡，你早就是滿分了，而且是永遠不會被扣分的那種滿分。

有時候我在想，如果能用牠看我的眼光，看我自己一次就好，
或許我也會對自己溫柔一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個對別人很好、卻總是對自己太嚴苛的人——讓他被狗的眼光提醒一次
variations: 狗是這世界上唯一，把你放在牠自己前面的生命。牠餓，也會先確認你有沒有吃飽。
你不相信自己值得被愛的那些夜裡，牠一直用行動告訴你：你值得，一直都值得。

en.content:
In this whole world, maybe only a dog
loves you more than you love yourself.

You'll decide you're not good enough, not thin enough, not successful enough.
You'll pick yourself apart in the mirror, and lie awake at night wondering if you even deserve to be loved.

But he has never doubted it for a single second.
In his heart you scored full marks long ago — the kind of full marks that can never be docked a point.

Sometimes I think: if I could see myself just once through his eyes,
maybe I'd be a little gentler with myself too.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who's endlessly kind to others but far too hard on themselves — let a dog's gaze remind them once
variations: A dog is the only creature on earth who puts you ahead of himself. Even when he's hungry, he checks first that you've eaten.
On all those nights you couldn't believe you were worth loving, he kept telling you, through everything he did: you are, you always have been.

items.sourceNote: 狗的社群追蹤數比主人多，改寫自英文網路梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我家狗的 IG 追蹤數，比我還多。

更誇張的是，牠一張躺在地上放空的照片，
按讚數是我精心修圖三小時的自拍的十倍。

我不是嫉妒，我只是……
開始認真思考，是不是該請牠當我的經紀人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個幫狗開了專屬帳號、然後發現狗比自己紅的朋友
variations: 我現在發文都要先問過牠——不是徵求同意，是想蹭一下牠的流量。
別人是狗仔隊跟拍明星，我是明星跟拍我家那隻狗。

en.content:
My dog has more followers than I do.

What really stings is that one photo of him zoned out on the floor
gets ten times the likes of a selfie I edited for three hours.

I'm not jealous. I'm just…
starting to seriously consider asking him to be my manager.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who made their dog a dedicated account and then watched the dog out-famous them
variations: These days I run every post by him first — not for approval, just hoping some of his reach rubs off on me.
Other people have paparazzi chasing celebrities. I'm the one chasing my dog around with a phone.

items.sourceNote: 床的所有權其實在狗手上，改寫自英文梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
感情狀態：睡覺睡得歪七扭八，只為了不吵到狗。

這張床名義上是我的，
實際上我只是租了角落那一小塊，
而且租約隨時會被房東縮短。

牠翻個身，我就得往懸崖再挪五公分。
我不是睡在床上，我是掛在床邊求生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明買了雙人床、卻每天睡成一條線的狗主人
variations: 我半夜醒來發現自己抱著棉被的邊角發抖，而牠四腳朝天躺在正中央，睡得像個國王。
床是我買的，棉被是我洗的，位置是牠選的——這個家的權力結構很清楚。

en.content:
Relationship status: sleeping in a twisted pretzel shape so I don't disturb the dog.

Technically this bed is mine.
In practice, I rent a small corner of it,
and the lease can be shortened by my landlord at any moment.

He rolls over, and I shuffle another two inches toward the edge.
I'm not sleeping on the bed. I'm clinging to the side of it for survival.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who bought a queen-size bed and still sleeps in a single straight line every night
variations: I woke up at 3 a.m. hugging one corner of the blanket while he lay spread-eagle in the dead center, sleeping like a king.
I bought the bed, I wash the sheets, he picks the spot — the power structure in this house is very clear.

items.sourceNote: 狗盯著你吃東西的每一口，改寫自英文梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你做的每一餐，你切的每一片，
你吃的每一口——牠都在看。

那不是普通的看，
是把你的手當成人生唯一希望的看。
是「我已經三天沒吃東西了」的看（牠十分鐘前才吃飽）。

你咬下第一口的瞬間，
牠的眼睛就變成兩顆會發光的黑寶石，
用整個靈魂問你：那個，可以分我一點嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個吃飯永遠有一雙眼睛盯著、吃到有罪惡感的狗主人
variations: 牠的表演能力足以拿下奧斯卡——每一餐都演出「被全世界遺忘的飢餓孤兒」。
我現在吃東西都要背對牠，不然那個眼神會讓我懷疑自己是不是很殘忍。

en.content:
Every meal you make, every slice you cut,
every bite you take — he's watching.

And it's not ordinary watching.
It's the watching of a creature who has decided your hand is his one hope in life.
It's the "I haven't eaten in three days" stare (he was fed ten minutes ago).

The instant you take that first bite,
his eyes turn into two glowing black gemstones,
asking you with his entire soul: hey… could I maybe have a little?
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who can never eat in peace and feels guilty by the second bite
variations: His acting range deserves an Oscar — every single meal, he performs 'the starving orphan the whole world forgot.'
I now eat with my back to him, because that stare makes me genuinely question whether I'm a cruel person.

items.sourceNote: 腦中有一半時間都在想狗，改寫自英文梗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
我看起來好像在認真思考人生大事。

其實我百分之九十九的腦容量，
都在想著等一下要回家摸狗。

重要會議是背景音，
深刻的對話是背景音，
連我自己的人生規劃都是背景音——

唯一清晰的畫面，
是牠聽到鑰匙聲、衝到門口那一刻的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個上班上到一半、突然開始想念家裡狗狗的人
variations: 別人下班是解脫，我下班是赴約——約會對象是一隻等了我一整天的狗。
我的專注力有限，而它幾乎全數被一隻毛茸茸的生物預訂走了。

en.content:
I may look like I'm deep in thought about life's big questions.

Really, ninety-nine percent of my brain
is just thinking about petting my dog when I get home.

The important meeting is background noise.
The meaningful conversation is background noise.
Even my own life plans are background noise —

the only vivid image in my head
is the way he bolts to the door the second he hears my keys.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who's halfway through the workday and suddenly aching to see their dog
variations: For other people, clocking out is relief. For me it's a date — with a dog who's been waiting all day.
My attention span is limited, and nearly all of it is booked solid by one fuzzy creature.

items.sourceNote: 狗不是生命的全部卻讓生命完整，改寫自 Roger Caras 概念，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗不會是你生命的全部，
牠只佔了你人生裡短短的一段時光。

但很奇怪，
就是這麼一段時光，
卻讓你原本破了一個角的日子，變得完整。

牠不懂你的工作，不懂你的煩惱，
卻懂得在你關燈之後，安靜地走過來，
把整個身體的重量，都靠在你身上——
好像在說：沒關係，今天過完了，我在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得日子過得零零碎碎、卻在養狗之後慢慢被拼回完整的人
variations: 牠陪你的時間不長，卻剛好補上了你人生裡那個一直空著的位置。
你給了牠一個家，牠給了你一段，之後不管過多久，想起來都會覺得溫暖的時光。

en.content:
A dog won't be your whole life.
He only takes up a short stretch of your years.

But here's the strange part:
it's exactly that short stretch
that takes your days — chipped at one corner — and makes them whole.

He doesn't understand your job or your worries,
yet somehow he knows to walk over quietly after you turn off the lights
and lean the full weight of his body against you,
as if to say: it's okay, today is over, I'm here.
en.editorNote: Send to the person whose days felt scattered and fragmented until a dog slowly pieced them back together
variations: His time with you isn't long, but it lands right in the one spot in your life that had been sitting empty.
You gave him a home. He gave you a stretch of time that, no matter how many years pass, will always feel warm to remember.

items.sourceNote: 領養浪浪、第二次機會主題，概念重寫為原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
領養牠的那天，我以為我在做好事。

後來才知道，
那天真正被撿回來的人是我。

牠帶著一身來歷不明的傷，
卻在第三天就決定要信任我；
我帶著一堆說不出口的難過，
花了三年才學會像牠那樣，重新相信一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寫在領養週年的貼文，或送給正在猶豫要不要開放心房的朋友
variations: 我以為我給了牠一個家，其實是牠讓那間房子終於變成家。
牠被丟下過一次，卻還是選擇每天在門口等我回來——這種勇氣，我學了好久。

en.content:
The day I adopted her, I thought I was doing a good deed.

It took me a while to realize
the one who actually got rescued that day was me.

She showed up with scars nobody could explain,
and still decided to trust me by day three.
I showed up with sadness I couldn't put into words,
and it took me three years to learn how to trust anything again the way she did.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an adoption-anniversary post, or for a friend who's deciding whether to let someone in again
variations: I thought I gave her a home. Really, she's the one who turned that apartment into one.
She was left behind once and still chooses to wait by the door for me every day. That kind of courage took me years to learn.

items.sourceNote: 老狗與領養老狗主題，概念重寫為原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養老狗，有點像是把一部電影從最好看的地方開始看。

牠不會咬你的鞋，不會半夜要你陪玩，
牠只是安靜地趴在你腳邊，
用一種「我這輩子看過的事情比你多」的表情，望著窗外。

白毛從嘴邊開始長，走路慢了半拍，
可是牠看你的眼神，一天比一天更慢、更用力——
好像很清楚，剩下的每一天都要看仔細一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合分享給家中有老狗、或正在考慮領養高齡犬的人
variations: 牠的腳步慢下來，只是為了讓你有時間，把牠好好記住。
最珍貴的東西通常都有點舊：舊沙發、舊朋友，還有一隻嘴邊都白了的老狗。

en.content:
Adopting a senior dog is a bit like starting a movie at the best part.

He won't chew your shoes or wake you up at 2 a.m. to play.
He just lies quietly by your feet,
watching the window with a look that says he's seen more of life than you have.

The white comes in around his muzzle, his walk slows half a beat,
but the way he looks at you gets slower and heavier every day —
like he knows every remaining one is worth studying closely.
en.editorNote: Share with someone whose dog is going gray, or who's weighing whether to adopt an older dog
variations: He slowed down so you'd have time to memorize him.
The best things are usually a little worn: the old couch, the old friends, and a dog with a white muzzle.

items.sourceNote: 「狗覺得我很酷，直到郵差出現」概念，重寫加長為原創段子

zh-tw.content:
在我家狗心中，我是世界第一強的男人。

我能打開零食櫃，能變出牽繩，能一手抱起牠三十公斤的身體（勉強）。

這個信仰維持得很好，
直到快遞按了電鈴。

那一秒，牠衝到門口狂吠，回頭看我一眼，
眼神清楚地寫著：「你退後，這裡交給我，你不行。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配上狗狗對著門口狂叫的照片，或分享給每天被快遞打斷居家辦公的人
variations: 我在我家狗眼中的地位，會隨著門鈴聲即時下降。
牠平常覺得我無所不能，一聽到電鈴就馬上把我降級成後勤人員。

en.content:
In my dog's eyes, I am the strongest man alive.

I can open the treat cabinet. I can summon the leash out of nowhere. I can lift all sixty pounds of him (barely).

This reputation held up beautifully —
right up until the delivery guy rang the bell.

In that one second he bolted to the door, barking, then glanced back at me
with a look that clearly said: "Stand back. I've got this. You're not built for it."
en.editorNote: Pair it with a photo of your dog losing it at the front door, or send to anyone whose work-from-home day gets derailed by deliveries
variations: My status in this house drops in real time, at the exact pitch of the doorbell.
He thinks I can do anything, until the bell rings and I'm instantly demoted to support staff.

items.sourceNote: 「我努力工作是為了狗能過更好的午睡人生」概念，重寫為原創段子

zh-tw.content:
我每天早上七點起床，通勤一小時，開三個會，加班到八點。

為的是什麼？

為了讓一隻從早睡到晚的生物，
可以睡在有恆溫空調的房間裡，
吃無穀低敏的飼料，
然後在我進門的時候，抬頭看我兩秒，翻個身繼續睡。

沒關係，值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下班打卡文、加班自嘲文，或任何一張狗在你工作時睡得四腳朝天的照片
variations: 我不是在上班，我是在幫我家狗賺退休金。
牠一天睡十八小時，我一天工作十小時，我們兩個裡面只有一個過得像人生勝利組。

en.content:
I get up at seven, commute for an hour, sit through three meetings, and clock out at eight.

All of it for what?

So that a creature who sleeps from sunrise to sunset
can nap in a climate-controlled room,
eat the grain-free hypoallergenic kibble,
and then, when I finally walk in the door, lift his head for two whole seconds before rolling over and going back to sleep.

Honestly? Worth it.
en.editorNote: Great for an end-of-workday post, or any photo of your dog passed out belly-up while you're on a call
variations: I'm not building a career. I'm funding my dog's retirement.
He sleeps eighteen hours a day, I work ten. Only one of us is winning at life, and it isn't me.

items.sourceNote: 「狗是唯一愛你勝過愛自己的生物」概念，重寫為原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
地球上大概只有狗，
會把自己排在你後面。

最後一塊肉，牠會讓你先吃；
打雷的晚上牠自己也怕，卻硬要擠到你身邊，好像是牠在保護你；
你生病躺著，牠不吵不鬧，就趴在床邊，一整天不走。

人類練了一輩子的無私，
牠出生就會了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配狗狗守在床邊的照片，或送給正在被毛孩無條件疼愛著的人
variations: 牠沒學過什麼叫無條件，牠只是天生就不會有條件。
全世界只有牠，會在自己也害怕的時候，先跑來確認你有沒有事。

en.content:
A dog might be the only creature on earth
that puts itself second to you.

The last bite of steak — she'll let you have it.
On a night of thunder she's terrified too, and still squeezes up against you like she's the one standing guard.
When you're sick in bed she doesn't whine or pace; she just lies on the floor beside you and refuses to leave all day.

Selflessness takes people a lifetime to practice.
She was born already knowing it.
en.editorNote: Pair with a photo of your dog keeping watch by the bed, or send to someone being loved unconditionally right now
variations: She never learned what "unconditional" means. She just never figured out how to have conditions.
She's the only one who, while scared herself, runs over first to check that you're okay.
